Operator Associate Savannah, GA We are looking for a Operator Associate to join our Catalyst team in Savannah, GA. Come create chemistry with us! BASF’s Catalysts division is the global market leader in catalyst technologies. The division develops and produces mobile emissions catalysts as well as process catalysts and offers precious metals trading, recycling and related products and services. As an Operator 1 for our Catalyst business unit, you will start a meaningful career at an entry-level position while supporting a division that is active developing technologies that protect the air we breathe, produce the fuels that power our world and ensure efficient production of a wide variety of chemicals, plastics and other products including advanced battery materials. Our portfolio serves industries such as the automotive, chemical sectors, refineries, battery manufacturers as well as the production of fuels, chemicals and plastics. The Savannah plant in particular manufactures and operates the following products: fluid catalytic cracking (FCC) catalysts, co-catalysts and additives used by petroleum refiners to convert crude oil into gasoline, other transportation fuels, heating oil, and petrochemical feed stocks. During your 12-hour rotating shift as a Operator Associate, you will Operate major plant equipment and/or processes (pumps, valves, filters, screens, compressors, dryers, etc.) following standard procedures and practices, as well as participate in system improvement processes. Maintain equipment and diagnoses problems to avoid shutdowns. Drive/Operate a Forklift. Keep records and interpret a variety of instructions furnished in written, oral, diagram, or schedule form. This includes the ability to rate, ratio, percent and to draw as well as interpret bar graphs. Responsible for product quantity and quality (including analytical testing). If you... Have a High School Diploma or GED. Have minimum of 2 years of experience in manufacturing and Operations. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact myhr@basf.com to report fraud. Process Engineer II - Chemicals Greenville, OH (Onsite) We are looking for an experienced Chemical Engineer to join our Coatings team in Greenville, OH. In this role, you will be responsible for providing technical support needed to execute the required production plan for Electrocoat Binder (Ecoat) production, which produces resins and intermediates for BASF’s CATHOGUARD products. You will also demonstrate strong leadership in a safe plant operation and play an important role in driving standardization across shifts, optimizing cycle times, and providing process expertise. Additionally, you will lead troubleshooting efforts and provide solutions when problems arise. This role will also require you to mentor the supporting Operations Engineer, and you will also be required to provide direction to the production lane to produce the processes you are responsible for safely, on quality, and efficiently. What you'll do... Partnering with Operations to maintain safe operation of Ecoat processes. Identifying and leading projects to debottleneck and/or otherwise improve cost and processes. Review and correct any formulation, quality of process issues. Providing troubleshooting support, including off-hours, to safely resume production during production issues. Clearly communicating processes, project information, and associated requirements to site and off-site personnel. Providing technical guidance and root cause analysis facilitation for investigations. Demonstrating technical responsibility for all plant trials in Ecoat. Delivering solutions for recurring process issues. If you... Have Bachelors in chemical engineering. Have over 8+ years of experience in the chemical industry. Self-motivated and focused on improving current procedures to become more efficient and expedient. Excellent computer skills and ABB automation knowledge preferred. Have familiarity with process equipment and instrumentation. Have solid problem-solving skills and experience in root cause failure analysis. Have a strong understanding of Process Safety Concepts and their applications and familiarity with EHS procedures/standards, including PSM. Have an excellent communication skills and interpersonal understanding to work cross functionally. Lead Electrical Engineer Greenville,OH Come create chemistry with us! We are seeking a professional like you to join our team.  As an Electrical / Instrumentation engineer at the Greenville site you will support a 24/7 operation by ensuring the site electrical infrastructure and process instrumentation is maintained in a safe and reliable state.  You will also propose upgrades of equipment to the latest technologies and lead site capital projects.  You will interact regularly with our maintenance team, various contractors and our other internal groups to complete a variety of projects. What you'll be doing: Ensuring operational safety and reliability through your expertise in electrical infrastructure, overseeing the performance and maintenance of systems such as switchgear, distribution panels, and lighting panels, while upholding arc flash compliance. Driving efficiency in repairs and inventory by leveraging your ability to interpret piping & instrumentation diagrams, engineering drawings, and technical documentation—ensuring critical spare parts are available and documentation is accurately maintained. Supporting project execution with your knowledge of planning and documentation, contributing to bid packages, construction records, and specification sheets for new electrical and instrumentation installations. Advancing automation initiatives by applying your understanding of DCS, PLCs, and VFDs to reduce process variability and enhance trend analysis across site operations. Fostering collaboration through your strong networking and coordination skills, working closely with procurement, contractors, suppliers, and production teams to align on technical and operational goals. Utilizing digital tools effectively by managing maintenance and procurement workflows in SAP, and using Microsoft Office and MS Project for communication, reporting, and planning. Mentoring and developing talent by guiding site instrument technicians in their daily tasks and supporting their long-term professional growth through your technical leadership. What we're looking for: Hold a Bachelor’s degree in electrical engineering , bringing a solid foundation in technical principles and applied problem-solving. Have deep knowledge of electrical distribution, instrumentation, and automation equipment , enabling you to support complex systems with confidence and precision. Are proficient in SAP, MS Project, and the Microsoft Office suite , ensuring you can manage maintenance workflows, project timelines, and reporting with ease. Have experience working in a PSM (Process Safety Management) environment , giving you an edge in navigating safety-critical operations. Excel at mentoring and communicating across all levels of an organization , fostering collaboration, knowledge sharing, and team development. Thrive in dynamic environments , where you can effectively manage non-routine and emergency situations such as schedule disruptions, plant shutdowns, startups, and commissioning activities. Mechanic I Attapulgus, GA We are looking for a Mechanic I to join our Catalyst team in Attapulgus, GA. Come create chemistry with us! BASF’s Catalysts division is the global market leader in catalyst technologies. The division develops and produces mobile emissions catalysts as well as process catalysts and offers precious metals trading, recycling and related products and services. The BASF Attapulgus, Georgia site manufactures catalyst for the global refining industry. The incumbent in this position will provide maintenance mechanical support to BASF Attapulgus Operations with a Safety First mindset.  The incumbent will be responsible for installing, maintaining, troubleshooting, and repairing industrial equipment. They will use a variety of hand tools, such as hammers, wrenches, sockets, pliers, and impacts for equipment repair; equipment for welding and cutting for fabrication; as well as calipers, measuring tapes, torque wrenches and lasers for precision maintenance. During your shift as a Mechanic I, you will Diagnose troubleshoot, plan and perform all mechanical operations necessary to modify, dismantle, repair industrial equipment Overhaul and repair all types of difficult and complicated plant machinery and equipment including centrifugal pumps, gearboxes, filter belts, fans and other machinery Process and special equipment involving layout and development Perform work in the mechanic's skill area such as fabrication, assembly, welding, burning, cutting and pipefitting. Inspect work for quality and dimensional requirements. If you... Have a HS Diploma or GED with 5 years of Industrial Mechanic experience OR have a 2 year Associate degree in Industrial Maintenance and 3 years of experience in an Industrial Mechanic position Are willing and able to work on-call as required (day/night/weekend/holidays/on call) Can lift up to 50 lbs, carrying up to 25 lbs. Chemical Operator Cincinnati, OH. Pay Range: Starting at $28 hourly - 24/7 | Rotating Shifts, overtime eligible! We are looking for a Chemical Operator to join our Care Chemicals (EM) team in Cincinnati, OH. Come create chemistry with us! As the leading global chemical supplier for the personal care, home care, industrial and institutional cleaning industries, and technical applications in the agrochemical industry, BASF Care Chemicals is focused on partnering with customers to create innovative solutions with great speed and agility to meet the current and future needs of society in a more sustainable way. We are proud to contribute to the long-term success of our customers’ brands by offering a broad range of products and concepts such as common household items, like cosmetics, detergents and cleaners, as well as industrial products and processes like crop protection, rubber belts, hoses, concrete, and metal plating. At Care Chemicals, we not only care about supporting our customers and achieving strong business results; we care about our team members. We aim to create a motivating, inspiring and inclusive work environment where all team members feel supported, heard and developed. Being part of our team means showing up every day to work collaboratively across functions to win for the overall success of the team. We value bringing diverse perspectives and backgrounds together to stretch our teams to think differently, have the courage to speak up and challenge the status quo, and problem solve for the greater good of our customers and society. We as the Care Chemicals division of BASF carry the passion to care in our DNA: we care for our customers, consumers, employees, society and our planet. Come, be a part of our team and create Care Chemicals with us. As a chemical operator you will be responsible for the safe and efficient operation of the production equipment inthe Cincinnati, OH plant. This includes operation of chemical processing equipment; including reactors, refining,and filtration systems. Also involves the transfer of material to and from other departments as necessary andloading of tank trucks and rail cars for customer shipments. During your rotating shift as a Chemical Operator, you will Load and unload tanks and rail cars and receipt of truck deliveries, in addition to perform collection of samplesfrom tank cars and vessels. Conduct visual inspection of operating equipment to determine current operating status whilst demonstrateeffective operations of the DCS in daily work environment. Read, record, and interpret data of instruments (reactors, tanks, pumps, filtration systems, etc.) and makecorrective adjustments as required for efficient production runs. Demonstrate proficiency at operating all production equipment, with minimal supervision, as well as use ofcomputer systems to complete work assignments. Participate in all required safety training and wear proper personal protective equipment when working in theplant area. Perform housekeeping to maintain production and operations in a neat and orderly condition. Work and communicate effectively with Supply Chain Planners, Logistics Planner, Production Coordinator,Maintenance, Lab, team members, etc. to accomplish high quality standards that meet/exceed customerrequirements. Support and adhere to the principles of the American Chemistry Council's Responsible Care®, EPA and OSHAinitiatives by protecting the environment, health, safety and security of our employees, contractors, carriers,distributors, visitors, customers, and neighbors. If you... Hold a High School Diploma or GED required. Preferrably possess a minimum 2 years working in a manufacturing environment. Bring DCS console operating knowledge and SAP (preferred). Possess excellent safety standards and strong troubleshooting skills. Maintenance Technician Savannah, GA We are looking for a Maintenance Technician to join our Catalyst team in Savannah, GA. Come create chemistry with us! BASF’s Catalysts division is the global market leader in catalyst technologies. The division develops andproduces mobile emissions catalysts as well as process catalysts and offers precious metals trading, recyclingand related products and services. The primary function of this job is to ensure proper maintenance within the facility and to ensure timely productionof highquality products in a cost efficient, safe and environmentally responsible manner. The job requirestechnical and problemsolving skills. Work activities include the repair and maintenance of the facility and its equipment. During your shift as a Maintenance Technician, you will Conducting routine and preventive maintenance on plant equipment and the facility in a timely and efficient manner, troubleshooting and resolving maintenance issues. Working independently, if necessary, to complete maintenance tasks and following written instructions. Proficiently utilizing hand tools, power tools, cutting and burning equipment, welding equipment, and operating material handling equipment. Maintaining a clean and organized work area, completing all necessary paperwork accurately and in a timely manner. Adhering to all appropriate Environmental Health and Safety (EHS) policies. Wearing prescribed Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) and ensuring that prescribed safeguards are properly used. Notifying the supervisor before undertaking a work activity for which you are not adequately trained. Promptly reporting any unsafe acts or conditions to the supervisor and taking immediate interim steps to protect yourself and others from serious risks. Understanding that Safety and Environmental responsibility is a significant aspect of BASF's Principles and promptly reporting all unsafe conditions and accidents to the appropriate manager, ensuring the safety of yourself and others. If you... Possess a High School Diploma or General Educational Diploma. A Technical Degree is preferred. Have a minimum of 3 years of experience in industrial maintenance. Demonstrate proficiency in precision maintenance on rotating manufacturing equipment. Have experience working with SAP. Are skilled in operating equipment such as forklifts, scissor lifts, and boom lifts. Account Representative - Automotive Refinish - Maryland, DC Maryland, Washington, D.C (Remote) **Field role / Company car provided / over night travel up to 30% We are looking for an Account Representative to join our Automotive Refinish team covering Maryland, Washington, D.C. and special projects within the Mid-Atlantic Region Come create chemistry with us! BASF Automotive Refinish Coatings Solutions offers high-quality refinish paint brands and paint-related products to body shops from around the world. Our solutions are approved by the world’s leading automotive manufacturers for the repair of their vehicles. We provide collision centers with a vast array of solutions that help drive their business performance and efficiency.  At the heart of our business are our people and our trainers who take care of our customers’ needs including car painting skills, advanced body shop management solutions, and sophisticated management solutions. As an Account Representative for Maryland and Washington, D.C , you will work with automotive collision repair shop customers as well as distribution partners to grow our market share sustainably.  You will be responsible to sell BASF automotive refinish products and services needed to support our collision center customers.  You will also provide support to our distribution partners in the region to ensure adequate market coverage and customer support to our end users. Digital Innovation Analyst Come create chemistry with us! BASF/Chemetall, is the Surface Treatment global business unit of BASF’s Coatings division, operating under the Chemetall brand, and a leading supplier of applied surface treatments worldwide. At BASF/Chemetall, we develop and manufacture tailor-made technology and system solutions for applied surface technology. Our products protect metals from corrosion, facilitate forming and treatment, prepare parts optimally for the painting process and ensure excellent coating adhesion. Our technologies and products are used in a variety of industries and end markets such as automotive, aerospace, aluminum finishing and metal forming. The chemical treatment of metal surfaces is BASF/Chemetall core competence. The focus of our worldwide activities is on the development and implementation of customized technology and system solutions for surface treatment. We are looking for a Digital Innovation Analyst to join our Digital Innovation Team in New Providence, NJ. As a Digital Innovation Analyst, you create chemistry by... Responsible for designing innovative technical solutions leveraging digital platforms, including Salesforce, SAP, Bizagi, Microsoft Power Platform. Carry configuration changes and maintenance as required to ensure quality and performance of the designed tools. Assist in end-to-end development of corporate service applications and support the implementation. Project the path forward for digitalization, take initiative to move outdated processes and develop a number of applications and workflows to modernize and improve these business processes. Serve as the technical lead for Salesforce marketing cloud (NA and APAC). Support in maintenance of SQL server on need basis to ensure data integrity and proper functioning. Up to 10% domestic travel required to various Chemetall offices and client sites nationwide. Local telecommuting permitted . If you have... Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Information Technology, or a related field and five (5) years of related experience required. Alternatively, the employer would accept a Master’s degree and two (2) years of related experience required. Two (2) years of experience must include: Salesforce CRM; SAP BW; MS SQL Server; Power BI/ Tableau; Power Apps; Power Automate; Bizagi; Microsoft Azure; Databricks; and Microsoft dataverse. Up to 10% domestic travel required to various Chemetall offices and client sites nationwide. Local telecommuting permitted. Lab Technician I Savannah, GA. We are looking for a Lab Technician o join our Catalyst team in Savannah, GA. Come create chemistry with us! BASF’s Catalysts division is the global market leader in catalyst technologies. The division develops andproduces mobile emissions catalysts as well as process catalysts and offers precious metals trading, recyclingand related products and services. As a Lab Technician for our Catalyst business unit, you will start a meaningful career and you will be responsible to Conduct quality control testing on products manufactured by the BASF Savannah site facility in accordance with documented analytical test procedures as directed by the Quality Supervisor. The Savannah plant in particular manufactures and operates the following products: fluid catalytic cracking (FCC) catalysts, co-catalysts and additives used by petroleum refiners to convert crude oil into gasoline, other transportation fuels, heating oil, and petrochemical feed stocks. During your 12-hour rotating shift as a Lab Technician I, you will Set up, calibrate, and operate laboratory instrumentation such as analytical balances, pH and ion meters, microwave moisture analysis ovens, microwave digestion ovens, surface area analysis, atomic absorption, X-ray fluorescence and diffraction and particle size analysis. Operate computer-controlled instrumentation and be able to store and extract data. Prepare solutions, standards, and buffers according to standard formulas used in analytical analysis. Prepare quality control production samples such as microspheres, zeolites, FCC catalysts and alumina. If you... Have a High School Diploma or GED with 2-3 years of laboratory experience. Have an Associate´s Degree/Bachelor ’ s degree in chemistry/biology or related field or any higher education: No experience required. Are familiar with Laboratory Analysis and math calculations. Are willing and able to work 12-hour shifts, rotating from day to night shifts. Can lift up to 50 lbs, carrying up to 25 lbs. description:

Software Engineering & Operations Manager Austin, TX We are looking for a Software Engineering & Operations Manager  to join our trinamiX team in Austin. Come create innovative solutions with us! trinamiX , a brand of BASF, is expanding into the United States to develop advanced sensing and biometric security solutions that unlock previously undiscovered opportunities. Our mission is to redefine what’s possible by delivering technologies that empower industries, enhance lives, and drive progress through precision, reliability, and innovation. We are dedicated to improving decision-making and security with cutting-edge technology. By pioneering advanced sensing technologies, we aim to shape a smarter, more connected future where our solutions pave the way for innovation and growth. As part of this vision, we are looking for an experienced Software Developer leader to guide our trinamiX team and ensure smooth local operations of our U.S. entity. As a Software Engineering & Operations Manager, you Foster Innovation by... Proactively identifying the best technical approach to meet strategic goals — we value engineers who think, not just execute. Performing rigorous code reviews and raising the technical bar across the engineering team. Translating technical and business requirements into high‑performance embedded C++ libraries. Designing and implementing robust, efficient, well‑tested, and extensible components across our core biometric authentication platform. Leading, managing, and mentoring a cross‑functional team of 11 Software Engineers and Data Scientists, with full disciplinary responsibility. Fostering a high‑performing, collaborative engineering culture focused on excellence and continuous improvement. Translating business requirements and product needs into clear technical direction for the team. Creating an environment where engineers grow, innovate, and do their best work. serving as the Site Leader of our Austin legal entity for administrative and legal compliance purposes. Overseeing day‑to‑day office operations to ensure a productive and well‑coordinated work environment. Maintaining regular communication and alignment with headquarters in Germany. Note: This Managing Director function is lightweight — no P&L ownership and no strategic business decision-making. If you... Hold a BS degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field. Bring 5+ years of professional software development experience. Have 4+ years of leadership experience with direct reports, incluiding disciplinary responsibility. Demonstrate strong, proven expertise in C++ (ideally C++17) and preferably Python 3. Have experience leading or managing a software engineering team. Exhibit excellent analytical thinking and communication skills in English. Are responsible, organized, and an exceptionally fast learner Have experience with Git, Linux, CMake, and/or QML.  (preferred) Are familiar with embedded systems or computer vision domains. Technical Sales Representative (ECT-Chemetall) Come create chemistry with us! BASF/Chemetall, is the Surface Treatment global business unit of BASF’s Coatings division, operating under the Chemetall brand, and a leading supplier of applied surface treatments worldwide. At BASF/Chemetall, we develop and manufacture tailor-made technology and system solutions for applied surface technology. Our products protect metals from corrosion, facilitate forming and treatment, prepare parts optimally for the painting process and ensure excellent coating adhesion. Our technologies and products are used in a variety of industries and end markets such as automotive, aerospace, aluminum finishing and metal forming. The chemical treatment of metal surfaces is BASF/Chemetall core competence. The focus of our worldwide activities is on the development and implementation of customized technology and system solutions for surface treatment We are looking for a Technical Sales Representative to join our Chemetall Team to grow business within the Allentown, PA and Scranton, PA  area. The Technical Sales Representative will contact established and prospective customers in an assigned territory for the purpose of selling Chemetall surface treatment chemicals. This individual will provide complete sales and service which ensures safe quality product performance while maximizing profitability within their assigned territory. This position offers a company car, laptop, phone, and all travel expenses covered by the company; travel can be anywhere from 50-60%. As a Technical Sales Representative (ECT-Chemetall), you create chemistry by... Understanding the business activities of accounts in the territory and applying knowledge of the customer’s internal operation to tailor sales presentations and sales proposals to the account’s specific needs. Managing territory profitability by reviewing territory compensation margins, providing margin improvement by product selection and optimum pricing.  Assures that technology provided within the territory strengthens the company’s competitive position within the accounts. Planning a weekly itinerary of calls to allocate time effectively and to ensure proper territory coverage by industry, geography and account potential. Calling on established and prospective customers, determines their needs and sells appropriate products to markets. Being able to physically service and call on existing and prospective customers. Making sales presentations, both orally and in written and answering customers’ questions concerning product use and providing pricing quotes. Attaining sales plan by securing new customers, increasing sales to current customers and renewing orders. Working closely with customers focusing on building strong business relationships. Maintaining contacts with customers in relation to their service requirements. Providing information on new products, methods, techniques and other general information such as safety and disposal. Training customers and insuring proper and safe use of Chemetall materials. Maintaining records of the activities in the territory as required by management and those records necessary for successful performance. Reporting competitive activities that impact sales in the territory. Preparing and submitting call reports, exception reports, and in-plant service reports, as required. Forwarding reports to appropriate business unit for inclusion into customer’s file. Improving technical knowledge of Chemetall materials through self-study and participation in professional societies. If you have... Bachelors Degree in Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, or Business is preferred. Minimum of 2 years of experience with business acumen, customer service, sales, account management, and territory managment is required. Outside B2B sales experience working with specialty chemicals / surface treatment (paint pretreatments, wastewater treatments, metalworking fluids, etc.) is preferred. Comfortable presenting and ability to conduct technical sales presentations and technical product trainings is required. Creating sales proposals and managing customer contracts is required. New Business Development: new customer acquisitions, "sales hunter" mentality while using continuous up/cross-selling techniques to increase sales growth within your territory. Act as a technical consultant for Chemetall products; will learn and understand Chemetall's product portfolio, product process, and the industries we service. Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal. Valid driver's license in good standing since a company car will be provided. Travel for this position is 50-60% with occasional overnight travel within your territory. Critical Rotating Equipment Engineer Freeport, TX We are looking for a Critical Rotating Equipment Engineer to join our GEC team in Freeport, TX. Come create chemistry with us! At BASF, we create chemistry through the power of connected minds. As the world’s leading chemical company, we help our customers in nearly every industry meet the current and future needs of society through science and innovation. The Global Engineering team leverages our technical know-how and expertise to deliver solutions that enable safe, sustainable, reliable, and cost-competitive production. As a Critical Rotating Equipment Engineer, you will provide support for BASF Corporation sites in the region and contribute to improvements as part of the BASF Machinery, Asset Maintenance and Reliability Community.  You will provide on-site support to continuously improve rotating equipment availability and assist in developing resolutions to bad actors or developing comprehensive maintenance strategies for critical equipment As a Critical Rotating Equipment Engineer, you create chemistry by... Leveraging your engineering education and experience with rotating equipment troubleshooting and repair to support manufacturing sites producing a wide range of products. Utilizing your depth of expertise to support plant maintenance and reliability teams by working together to diagnose issues, develop, and implement repairs and corrective actions for planned and emergency repairs, overhauls and outages. Supporting sites in the region by providing rotating equipment expertise to drive sustainable improvements as well as utilizing a forward thinking approach that embraces new technology and innovation as enablers to superior performance. Leading and participating in site Reliability Programs including (but not limited to) Bad Actor Program, Failure Mode and Effects Analysis, Root Cause Failure Analysis, Reliability Centered Maintenance, Risk Based Maintenance, etc. Working with related functions of BASF Engineering and Maintenance globally to align on work processes, procedures, methodologies, best practices, etc. and engaging across the region, you will collaborate with production, maintenance, engineering, and research personnel at multiple sites and business units. Act as a technical resource to develop comprehensive maintenance programs, operator training, and contract scope associated with critical refrigeration and chiller systems that support industrial applications.  This will entail contract management and engagement with the responsible contractors. If you... Hold a Bachelor's Degree in Engineering is required. Mechanical or Chemical Engineering is preferred. Possess 5 years of experience in rotating equipment troubleshooting and repair to support manufacturing sites. Have the ability to make crucial business decisions and recommendations to identify areas for improvement and increase equipment reliability. Have experience providing input on capital projects is a plus. description:

Senior Control Systems Engineer Freeport, TX (Onsite) Annual bonus We are looking for a Senior Control Systems Engineer to join our Petrochemicals (CP) team in Freeport, TX. Come create chemistry with us! BASF’s Petrochemical division is the starting point of BASF's petrochemical value creation worldwide, also known as “the heart of the Verbund,” representing the world's largest chemical markets. Together, let us keep the heart beating one creative solution at a time! As a Senior Control Systems Engineer and member of the Acrylic Acid Process Management team in Freeport, you will ensure the unit’s control systems operate safely, reliably, and efficiently. You will lead control system lifecycle and migration efforts, guide maintenance strategies for automation assets, and collaborate closely with the Operations, Technology, and Maintenance teams to deliver consistent reliability and enable future improvements. As a Senior Control Systems Engineer, you create chemistry by... Leading configuration, preventive maintenance, hardware/software support, and overall administration of all control systems within the Acrylic Acid units, this includes programming HMI, DCS and (Safety) PLCs using function block diagrams, ladder logic, and sequential function charts. Supporting daily operations by troubleshooting, tuning, and optimizing PID control loops, including leading root cause failure analyses and implementing corrective actions for control system issues. Supporting capital and maintenance projects involving control system interfaces through the design, implementation, and commissioning phases, including serving as the Manufacturing Representative or MOC Coordinator for selected projects Developing long-term control system lifecycle plans for the unit in alignment with the Operations and Asset Management teams. In line with this you will maintain detailed logic drawings and system configuration to support troubleshooting, audits, and lifecycle planning. Tracking total cost of ownership for control systems by monitoring failures, tracking maintenance contracts, optimizing software upgrades, and coordinating with vendors. Participating in peer groups with other Control Systems Engineers, Instrument & Electrical Maintenance Engineers, Technologists and Technicians to drive standardization of control systems across the Freeport site and within the unit. If you... Have a bachelor's degree in Computer, Chemical, Electrical Engineering or related engineering majors. Possess 7+ years of experience with control systems, automation, and instrumentation including DCS and/or PLC programming (Foxboro DCS, Triconex, Allen-Bradley PLC preferred). Demonstrate strong communication, interpersonal, and organizational skills, along with a keen attention to detail. Are prepared to respond to critical after-hours events that may impact EHS or production. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. Sr. Occupational Nurse Practitioner (Freeport, TX) Freeport, TX Relocation assistance is offered for applicable candidates. We are looking for a Sr. Occupational Nurse Practitioner (Freeport, TX) to join our NAF team in Freeport, TX. Come create chemistry with us! At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. We combine economic success with environmental protecion and social responsibility. The Sr. Occupational Nurse Practitioner at BASF Chemical, Freeport, TX location is responsible for overseeing the occupational health programs at the Freeport site with focus on identifying and controlling the risks arising from physical, chemical, and other workplace hazards with the goal of establishing and maintaining a safe and healthy working environment. As a Sr. Occupational Nurse Practitioner (Freeport, TX), you create chemistry by... Conducting employee health assessments—including pre-employment and annual physical examinations, medical history reviews, and fitness-for-work evaluations—while maintaining detailed and confidential medical records in accordance with BASF standards and regulatory requirements. Developing and implementing workplace health and safety programs that help prevent occupational illnesses and injuries, collaborating closely with site leadership, EHS, and HR to strengthen health and safety policies and initiatives. Providing medical care and emergency response support, including first aid and clinical care for employees who become ill or injured at work, supporting the site’s emergency medical response team, and participating in emergency planning and response situations such as chemical spills or releases. Supporting employee health and wellbeing through education and counseling on topics such as ergonomics, stress management, and mental health, while coordinating wellness initiatives including health screenings, health fairs, immunization clinics, and other health promotion activities. Monitoring and managing the health status of employees returning to work after work-related injuries or illnesses, supporting Worker’s Compensation processes where applicable, and advocating for safe and healthy working conditions across the site. Interpreting occupational health data such as spirometry and audiometric testing results, applying findings in clinical decision-making, and developing working knowledge of site-specific hazards and chemicals including Ammonia, Formaldehyde, Benzene, Acrylonitrile, and other relevant substances. Collaborating with BASF Corporate Medical, management, EHS, Human Resources, the Leave Management Team, and external healthcare partners—including designated collaborating physicians—to ensure alignment with BASF medical protocols, regulatory compliance, and best practices in occupational health. Supporting the occupational health nursing team in daily operations by serving as a second-call escalation resource for serious injuries or illnesses, rotating after-hours telephonic on-call responsibilities, assisting in the development of site medical directives and protocols, conducting annual plant tours with reporting to Corporate Medical, and stepping into the Occupational Nursing role when needed. If you... Hold current licensure as a Nurse Practitioner (NP) in the state of Texas or Texas license eligible with licensure in at least one state, preferably a compact state. Are proficient in Microsoft Office applications including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint. Demonstrate strong communication, counseling, and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work collaboratively with employees, leadership, and healthcare professionals. Preferred: Emergency Room and triage experience. Are proficient in electronic medical record systems. Experience working as an Occupational Nurse Practitioner, Occupational Health Nurse, or in a similar occupational health role. Possess knowledge of workplace health and safety principles and OSHA regulations, along with strong understanding of occupational and environmental health regulations and standards. Have experience in occupational and environmental health management within the chemical industry or a related manufacturing environment. Hold certification in Occupational Health Nursing (COHN) or a similar credential. Surfactants Lab Team Lead Geismar, LA The world needs [creative] solutions. We need YOU! Where the chemistry happens… We are seeking a professional like you to join our team.  In this role, you will be responsible for leading Geismar’s Surfactants Laboratory in a safe and efficient manner.  Monitoring of laboratory Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) will be key to ensure that areas of strength are maintained, and continuous improvement opportunities are identified and pursued.  The position will lead a Current Good Manufacturing Practices (cGMP) and ISO 9001-certified laboratory that must adhere to regulatory quality requirements including, but not limited to: Food & Drug Administration guidance for Active Pharmecutical Ingredients (API), ISO 9001 systems, EXCiPACT, Responsible Care Management System (RCMS), Halal, and Kosher.  The position has organizational responsibility for leading and developing a laboratory team of (1) Technologist, (1) Continuous Improvement-Validation Specialist, and multiple Quality Control Technicians. As a Surfactants Lab Team Lead, Your attention to detail and background in analytical chemistry will serve you well in leveraging Statistical Process Control and developing KPIs for determining areas of strength, as well as opportunities for improvement Your strong knowledge of regulatory principles is desirable for supporting compliance efforts for various regulatory standards, including but not limited to: Food & Drug Administration guidance for Active Pharmecutical Ingredients (API), ISO 9001 systems, EXCiPACT standard, and Responsible Care Management System A solid customer focus/solutions mindset and ability to communicate effectively at multiple organizational levels will afford you opportunities to evaluate sample methodologies and work processes to ensure that needs of internal business partners and external customers are consistently exceeded Your organizational responsibilities for a Technologist, a Continuous Improvement Specialist, and multiple Technicians will enable you to drive long-term sustainable improvements while instilling day-to-day fundamentals such as equipment troubleshooting, standards/controls compliance, etc. A creative mindset will be a welcomed attribute for purposes of identifying innovative approaches to historically standard work practices Your ability to collaborate will allow you to partner with key stakeholders for purposes of developing department-wide objectives and team-specific goals in support of those objectives Your ability to visualize the abstract will enable you to develop a strategic vision for your team Agility will serve you well in your interactions with your front-line team, staff professionals, business group peers, as well as functional and business group senior leadership Privacy Statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. OEM Site Service Representative Flint, MI 3rd Shift 10pm - 6am We are looking for a professional like you to join our team! This position supports the daily operation of BASF products in Customer Automotive Assembly Plants by providing on-site technical and application knowledge. The incumbent will demonstrate the technical and professional expertise to enhance BASF’s reputation with the customer. S/he will integrate within the business and technical community to bring additional value to ECO coatings customers. The role controls key performance indicators within product service environments to drive problem resolution and product improvement initiatives critical to customer performance expectations. What you'll do: Supporting the daily operation of BASF products in Customer Automotive Assembly Plants. Providing on-site technical and application expertise of product performance including color management, defect identification, and corrective action if necessary. Assuring product performance meets or exceeds customer expectations and supports business objectives to be our customer’s supplier of choice. Responding to customer requirements, enforcing product quality/performance specification, and supporting new product/application development. Supports the efforts of a BASF sales/technical team to assure value to the customer for product technical support, problem investigation, analysis, and resolution. Managing existing relationships and developing new ones. If you... An ingrained safety mindset, excellent interpersonal and communication skills will serve you well as you perform your daily tasks individually and in a team environment. You will employ your strong mechanical aptitude as you collect and analyze technical data to identify and resolve issues to provide a superior product to your customers. Accompanying your attention to detail in following written procedures, work instructions and JSAs, proficiency with the Microsoft Office product suite will be crucial to your effectiveness in communicating results. Your track record of working independently will demonstrate your leadership potential and flexibility that showcases a willingness to work shifts and weekends when necessary. Global Technical and Process Development Manager Freeport, TX We are looking for a Global Technical and Process Development Manager to join our Petrochemicals team in Freeport, TX. Come create chemistry with us! BASF’s Petrochemical division is the starting point of BASF's petrochemical value creation worldwide, also known as “the heart of the Verbund,” representing the world's largest chemical markets. Together, let us keep the heart beating one creative solution at a time! We are seeking a professional like you to join our team.  As a Global Technical and Process Development Manager for Superabsorbent Polymer (SAP) at BASF in Freeport, TX, you will be responsible for driving process innovation and optimizing technology across multiple world-scale plants, particularly in Freeport (FP) and Camacari (CA). You will collaborate with research and development (R&D) teams, manufacturing units, and customer-facing teams to ensure new products are introduced efficiently, profitably, and sustainably. This role will bridge laboratory innovations and large-scale manufacturing, ensuring customer requirements are met while maintaining process quality and safety. As a Global Technical and Process Development Manager, you create chemistry by... Leading efforts to drive process innovation in kneader technology as part of the global technology team. Being responsible for the operations of the ORP-10 lab and influential leading of the lab support. Scaling up learnings from lab-scale and pilot plant operations to world-scale plants in Freeport (FP) and Camacari (CA), ensuring alignment with production standards and customer needs. Collaborating within the global team to ensure new product introductions meet customer requirements, profitability, and sustainability targets. Providing on-demand technical guidance to manufacturing units, ensuring optimal process and quality steering. Driving and securing long-term technical know-how within the Superabsorbent Polymer community through training, coaching, documentation updates and knowledge exchange between global sites Participating as an active member of the global R&D and technical communities, promoting continuous learning and improvement. Leading the management of the process lab, ensuring safe and efficient operations in compliance with local and BASF regulations. Cultivating a motivating team atmosphere by leading through influence, ensuring the team remains customer-focused and aligned with broader business objectives. Overseeing the execution of testing, preparation of reports, and communication of feedback to various teams (Global Technology, R&D, etc.). Acting as the primary contact for local process and asset management, ensuring continuous compliance and risk mitigation. If you have... A PhD and 10+ years of experience or 15+ years of lab and technical experience in a global development and manufacturing environment in the chemical, polymer, or related industries. QA and Lab experience is a plus. Strong knowledge of technology, process scaling, process safety, and quality assurance practices. Experience with OPEX, digitization, and sustainability projects. Proven ability to perform on a cross-functional team and cultivate a collaborative, customer-centric atmosphere. Excellent communication skills, with the ability to present complex technical information Strong analytical skills with a practical approach to troubleshooting and process improvement. Ability to work in a global team and manage projects across multiple regions. Facilities Assistant (ECT-Chemetall) Come create chemistry with us! BASF/Chemetall, is the Surface Treatment global business unit of BASF’s Coatings division, operating under the Chemetall brand, and a leading supplier of applied surface treatments worldwide. At BASF/Chemetall, we develop and manufacture tailor-made technology and system solutions for applied surface technology. Our products protect metals from corrosion, facilitate forming and treatment, prepare parts optimally for the painting process and ensure excellent coating adhesion. Our technologies and products are used in a variety of industries and end markets such as automotive, aerospace, aluminum finishing and metal forming. The chemical treatment of metal surfaces is BASF/Chemetall core competence. The focus of our worldwide activities is on the development and implementation of customized technology and system solutions for surface treatment. We are looking for a Facilities Assistant to join our team in New Providence, NJ. The Facilities Assistant provides day‑to‑day administrative, coordination, and operational support to the Site Manager and facility leadership team. This role helps ensure smooth site operations by managing purchase orders, scheduling vendors and contractors, maintaining compliance documentation, supporting health and safety programs, and assisting with supervision of facility staff. The ideal candidate is detail‑oriented, organized, and able to effectively communicate with employees, vendors, and leadership. As a Facilities Assistant (ECT-Chemetall), you create chemistry by... Administrative & Operational Support Prepare, submit, and track purchase orders, invoices, and procurement requests. Assist in managing vendor and contractor scheduling, site access, and work coordination. Maintain site calendars, work schedules, facility service requests, and project timelines. Safety, Compliance & Policy Management Issue and document Safe Work Permits (SWP), ensuring contractor and employee compliance with safety requirements. Ensure building, safety, environmental, and Responsible Care® policies remain current, accurate, and compliant with corporate and regulatory standards. Assist with updating Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), emergency response plans, and compliance documentation. Monitor compliance tasks, audits, and corrective action items, ensuring timely completion. Facility & Staff Support Assist the Site Manager supporting facility staff and contractors. Help oversee daily operations to ensure safe, productive, and efficient workflow. Support facility maintenance activities by coordinating preventive maintenance schedules and service requests. Communicate effectively with site personnel regarding updates, and policy changes. Provide backup support to Shipping/Receiving – loading, unloading, labeling boxes, etc. Vendor & Contractor Management Serve as a point of contact for external vendors and service providers. Verify contractors meet site safety, documentation, and compliance requirements. Assist EHS department with contractor onboarding, work permits, and daily coordination during on‑site activities. Maintain training records and track employee certifications. Continuous Improvement Identify opportunities to improve administrative processes, documentation accuracy, and operational efficiency. Support site initiatives such as safety campaigns, audits, EHS inspections, and facility improvement projects. If you have... High school diploma or equivalent; associate degree or bachelor’s degree preferred. 2+ years of experience in facility operations, administrative support, project coordination, or similar roles. Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook, Teams). Excellent organizational, communication, and time‑management skills. Ability to work independently, multitask, and manage competing priorities. On-site position located in New Providence, NJ. Preferred Experience in manufacturing, facilities management, construction, chemical operations, or environmental health and safety. Knowledge of Responsible Care® principles or similar compliance frameworks. Familiarity with purchase order systems (SAP, Oracle, etc.) and vendor management processes (Avetta). Experience assisting with safety programs, SOPs, or training administration. Core Competencies Attention to Detail Communication & Collaboration Problem‑Solving Safety & Compliance Mindset Initiative & Ownership Customer Service Orientation Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. Process Engineer Sparta, TN We are looking for a Process Engineer to join our Performance Materials team in Sparta, TN. Come create chemistry with us! The Performance Materials division is at the forefront of the much-needed sustainability transformation in plastics. Our experts co-create products with customers to bring innovations to major industry sectors such as transportation, consumer goods, industrial applications, and construction. These solutions contribute to a sustainable future by pushing the boundaries in thermal resistance, robustness and lightweight applications. As a Process Engineer within the compounding manufacturing operation, you play a key role in ensuring safe, stable, and efficient production of polymer materials. You drive process optimization, troubleshoot manufacturing issues, support product qualifications and scale‑ups, and lead continuous improvement initiatives that enhance safety, quality, reliability, and cost performance in close collaboration with production and engineering teams. As a Process Engineer, you create chemistry by... Driving process efficiency by optimizing rates, yield, uptime, quality, safety, and overall equipment performance across mechanical and manual operations. Troubleshooting and resolving manufacturing and compounding issues, including root cause analysis and corrective actions for process, quality, and customer-related concerns. Leading process optimization and continuous improvement initiatives to simplify processes, reduce cost, improve productivity, and standardize best practices. Managing product qualifications and scale-ups, including capability trials, product family optimizations, and introduction of new or modified products. Leading and supporting capital and improvement projects, including equipment design, specifications, modifications, safety improvements, and waste reduction initiatives. Providing technical leadership and support by supervising Process Engineering Specialists and guiding Engineering Technicians and Production teams. Ensuring process stability and documentation accuracy by maintaining process sheets, operating conditions, and supporting SPC-driven process control. Supporting site and network-wide manufacturing strategies by sharing best practices, supporting other sites as needed, and contributing to technology, reliability, and manufacturing portfolio initiatives. If you... Hold a Bachelor’s degree in Chemical, Mechanical, or [Polymer] Engineering required,  have a minimum of 2 years of experience in manufacturing or process engineering preferred. Have experience leading and providing technical guidance to engineering technicians, specialists, or production teams. Demonstrate strong knowledge and practical application of SPC, root cause analysis, and continuous improvement methodologies (Six Sigma GB/BB preferred). Be proficient in process analysis and statistical tools such as Minitab and ASPEN Process Explorer, with the ability to translate data into actionable improvements. Communicate effectively by reading, writing, analyzing, and clearly conveying technical information, procedures, and reports in a cross-functional manufacturing environment. Meet physical requirements necessary to work safely in an industrial manufacturing environment, including standing for extended periods and working at heights or in confined spaces. Regional Technical Project Manager - Electrocoat (ECT-Chemetall) Come create chemistry with us! BASF/Chemetall, is the Surface Treatment global business unit of BASF’s Coatings division, operating under the Chemetall brand, and a leading supplier of applied surface treatments worldwide. At BASF/Chemetall, we develop and manufacture tailor-made technology and system solutions for applied surface technology. Our products protect metals from corrosion, facilitate forming and treatment, prepare parts optimally for the painting process and ensure excellent coating adhesion. Our technologies and products are used in a variety of industries and end markets such as automotive, aerospace, aluminum finishing and metal forming. The chemical treatment of metal surfaces is BASF/Chemetall core competence. The focus of our worldwide activities is on the development and implementation of customized technology and system solutions for surface treatment. We are looking for a Regional Technical Project Manager - Electrocoat to join our Technical Team in Jackson, MI. The Regional Technical Project Manager Electrocoat is a North America (US/CAN/MX) position, reporting to Head of Technology NA, aligning within the Key Industry Sales Group and RTS team. As a Regional Technical Project Manager - Electrocoat (ECT-Chemetall), you create chemistry by... Some of the responsibilities of the Regional Technical Project Manager are as follows: Primary Responsibilities Applications and Technical Service: Responsible for coordinating PPAP, Approval and customer electrocoat projects between New Providence, Southfield, BMT and other outside labs and suppliers. Projects will be primarily of a regional nature but with some global testing as well. Includes project submission, data review, report writing and sample review Responsible for sample preparation for submissions and communication of results to internal stakeholders. Will create and communicate timelines and prioritization of projects along with any resources required to complete projects. Corrosion Testing: Prepare and organize corrosion testing projects, ensuring compliance with documented test procedures. Handle samples and perform analyses according to established methods and instructions. Familiar with product approvals process according to customer specifications. Creation and management of databases containing surrogate data and approval status of various products. Will notify stakeholders when surrogate data is available for use in reporting. Writes clear, concise lab reports on work completed, making appropriate recommendations with the assistance of the Analytical Specialist and other technical resources. Management of lab and testing costs for both internal and external testing. Potential for sample preparation for both pretreatment and electrocoat using pilot line, includes bath building and make up along with analytical sample preparation. à Continuously upgrade technical skills by working in the laboratories with other technical personnel. Polishes written and verbal communication with technical and business stakeholders Supporting and guiding role in relevant audits Works in the laboratory in a safe and clean manner. If you have... BS Degree is required; preferably in a science or engineering field with equivalent experience/training. Minimum of 5 years of technical electrocoat and pretreatment experience required. Team Leader: people development, as well as technical skill development and cross training. Knowledge and understanding of Quality Management Guidance, ISO 9001, ISO 17015, etc. Prior experience with Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) is preferred. Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written. Interface management, ability to work with sales, customers, and technology. Multicultural mindset, working with colleagues from the U.S., Canada, and Mexico; ability to fluently speak English; ability to speak Spanish and/or French is preferred. Travel: up to 25% travel, this includes local and overnight travel. Physical Requirements: Includes sitting, walking, standing, alternate sit/stand, and viewing of monitor. Simple grasping frequently, power grasping, fine manipulation with hands, pushing, and pulling with hands, and ability to reach above shoulder level. Lifting and carrying up to 50lbs. Logistics Technician CDL Malcom, IA We are looking for a Logistics Technician CDL to join our Performance Materials team in Malcom. Come create chemistry with us! The Performance Materials division is at the forefront of the much-needed sustainability transformation in plastics. Our experts co-create products with customers to bring innovations to major industry sectors such as transportation, consumer goods, industrial applications, and construction.These solutions contribute to a sustainable future by pushing the boundaries in thermal resistance, robustness and lightweight applications. As a Logistics Technician at the Malcom, IA site, you will be responsible for operating forklifts and semi-trucks in a safe manner to ensure the efficient and timely movement of materials between warehouses and production plants. This role includes the use of computer systems to process documentation, track inventory movement, and support internal workflows to ensure complete and accurate records. Success in this role requires strong communication across multiple work groups which include operations, planning and quality. During your 8-hour day shift as a Logistics Technician CDL, you will Work with operations to ensure material is delivered to the production plants in a timely manner. Complete paperwork for necessary internal transfers. Operate a semi-truck. Operate a forklift to load and unload trucks. Band and shrink wrap drums. Label containers as required. Complete other required paperwork in an accurate and timely manner. If you... Possess a high school diploma or GED with at least 6 months of experience in a manufacturing or warehouse setting (preferred). Are Class A CDL certified Hold good computer skills. Have Forklift experience (preferred) and/or the ability to learn how to operate a forklift. Possess attention to detail, good communication and time management skills. Are able to work with little direction. Are able and willing to lift up to 50lbs on ocasion. Production Apprentice Sparta, TN Starting pay $20.00/hr Earn a paycheck while you learn a new career as a Production Technician Apprentice in Sparta, TN. Come create chemistry with us! At BASF, we are proud to introduce the next generation of Production Technician to the innovative world of Manufacturing. If you are looking to build a meaningful career while making an impact on the world, then come create chemistry with us! As a Production Technician in our Sparta, TN Nylon 6, Nylon 6/6, and Polyester colored specialty resins plant, you will make an impact on the world with a fundamental focus on sustainability. As a Production Technician, you will be a part of a team that manufactures products for use in the automotive, consumer/industrial, and power distribution industries. The products produced at Sparta, TN site can be found in everyday products like an auto interior, mirror, window housings, power tool housings, furniture applications, electrical switches, wall plates and more! As your apprenticeship begins, you will enroll in and attend a BASF-specific Production Technician program at Motlow State College. You will work and learn on-site at our Sparta, TN facility, which produces Nylon 6, Nylon 6/6, and Polyester colored specialty resins, guided by some of the best professionals in the industry. During your time here, we want you to focus on your learning without worrying about expenses, so BASF will cover the entire cost of your educational program, including textbooks! When you're not studying you will have opportunities to... Work hands-on in our Sparta, TN plant learning to: Conduct routine safety and equipment inspections and utilize various reporting mechanisms to rectify issues. Record proper operating and material information for production lot processing. Operate manufacturing equipment according to production ISO/TS16949 procedures. Follow process set-up instruction sheets, blend instruction sheets, etc. Prepare packaging for finished goods with proper labeling and materials identification. Gather, stage, and load proper materials for product compounding. Collect samples for QC and color testing. Perform housekeeping to maintain production and operations in neat and orderly condition. Operate peripheral and auxiliary equipment such as fume collection system, dust collection system, bulk pellet transfer systems, etc. Participate in problem-solving to make necessary equipment repairs or process corrections to maintain required production quality and volume. Operate a forklift to move needed raw materials and scrap materials to properly designated areas. Provide production or operations support as directed by Shift Supervisor or other management. Complete audits and inspections (total productive maintenance, housekeeping checklists, etc.). Complete computer based self-assessment. Complete required monthly training (classroom training, computer-based training, etc.). Troubleshoot problems in support areas. Complete paperwork with minimal amounts of errors. If you... Possess a High School Diploma or GED. Can learn on the job and use provided equipment/tools. Are able to follow both written and verbal procedures/instructions. Can follow operational and safety guidelines. Work independently and as part of a team. Have the ability to lift at least 50lbs. Technical Sales Representative (ECT-Chemetall) Come create chemistry with us! BASF/Chemetall, is the Surface Treatment global business unit of BASF’s Coatings division, operating under the Chemetall brand, and a leading supplier of applied surface treatments worldwide. At BASF/Chemetall, we develop and manufacture tailor-made technology and system solutions for applied surface technology. Our products protect metals from corrosion, facilitate forming and treatment, prepare parts optimally for the painting process and ensure excellent coating adhesion. Our technologies and products are used in a variety of industries and end markets such as automotive, aerospace, aluminum finishing and metal forming. The chemical treatment of metal surfaces is BASF/Chemetall core competence. The focus of our worldwide activities is on the development and implementation of customized technology and system solutions for surface treatment We are looking for a Technical Sales Representative to join our Chemetall Team to grow business within Cincinnati, OH, Louisville, KY, and Lexington, KY area. The Technical Sales Representative will contact established and prospective customers in an assigned territory for the purpose of selling Chemetall surface treatment chemicals. This individual will provide complete sales and service which ensures safe quality product performance while maximizing profitability within their assigned territory. This position offers a company car, laptop, phone, and all travel expenses covered by the company; travel can be anywhere from 50-60%. As a Technical Sales Representative (ECT-Chemetall), you create chemistry by... Understands the business activities of accounts in the territory and applies knowledge of the customer’s internal operation to tailor sales presentations and sales proposals to the account’s specific needs. Manages territory profitability by reviewing territory compensation margins, providing margin improvement by product selection and optimum pricing.  Assures that technology provided within the territory strengthens the company’s competitive position within the accounts. Calls on established and prospective customers, determines their needs, and sells appropriate products to markets, targeted by the business units of the company. Selects the process and the right material to solve customer cleaning, sanitation or surface treatment problems. Price negotiation: Provide pricing quotes, price and delivery policy within established limits, subject to approval. Attain sales plan by securing new customers, increasing sales to current customers and maintaining up to date opportunity pipeline in CRM (Salesforces). Works closely with customers focusing on building strong business relationships. Maintains contacts with customers in relation to their service requirements. Provides information on new products, methods, techniques and other general information such as safety and disposal. Trains customers and insures proper and safe use of Chemetall materials. Reports competitive activities, that impact sales in the territory, to the supervisor (District Manager). Prepares and submits call reports, exception reports, and in-plant service reports via CRM (Salesforce). Share reports with appropriate colleagues to ensure collaboration with different functions to keep all necessary information on all accounts. If you have... BS Degree in Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, Engineering, or Business is required. Minimum of 2 years of relatable experience is required. B2B/outside industrial sales experience, in specialty chemical sales (surface treatment-paint pretreatments, wastewater treatments, metalworking fluids, etc.) preferred. Ability to make sales presentations and follow up with written proposals and manage customer contracts. Well organized with sales planning. Managing customer accounts within the assigned territory. Strong selling skills for customer acquisition and continuous up-/cross-selling opportunities (customer support/account management) Build and strengthen customer relationships. Technical consulting capabilities regarding products and processes; will learn and understand Chemetall product portfolio. Strong business acumen. Strong Negotiation skills Problem solving skills Time management Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal. Travel up to 50-60%, with occasional overnight travel. Valid driver's license in good standing. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. description:
