Product Safety Stewardship Coordinator Come create chemistry with us! BASF/Chemetall, is the Surface Treatment global business unit of BASF’s Coatings division, operating under the Chemetall brand, and a leading supplier of applied surface treatments worldwide. At BASF/Chemetall, we develop and manufacture tailor-made technology and system solutions for applied surface technology. Our products protect metals from corrosion, facilitate forming and treatment, prepare parts optimally for the painting process and ensure excellent coating adhesion. Our technologies and products are used in a variety of industries and end markets such as automotive, aerospace, aluminum finishing and metal forming. The chemical treatment of metal surfaces is BASF/Chemetall core competence. The focus of our worldwide activities is on the development and implementation of customized technology and system solutions for surface treatment. Chemetall U.S., Inc. is seeking a Product Safety Stewardship Coordinator at our New Providence, NJ location to support BASF Chemetall business group by assuring product compliance to International, Federal, State and local regulations. As a Product Safety Stewardship Coordinator, you create chemistry by... Support sales and marketing initiatives and day-to-day business operations. Communicate and work closely with BASF Groups. Apply core set of knowledge in the SAP systems and the interactions between modules; operate in a cross-functional environment with significant involvement with Business Marketing & Supply Chain, operating/ Manufacturing plants, Sampling, Customer Service teams both in the North American region as well as with global contacts in Europe, Asia Pacific and Central & South America. Involved with the UN Global Harmonization System (GHS) to support exports for Europe and Asia Pacific regions. Responsible for creation, updating and reviewing of OSHA, WHMIS and NOM compliant Safety Data Sheets (SDS) and Labels. Assign NFPA 704 and HMIS hazard ratings to products. Apply knowledge in Biocide regulations FIFRA and PMRA. Responsible for regulatory review for new product introductions to North America. Address internal and external requests on NA regulatory affairs. Responsible for dangerous goods article release for 49CFR, TDG, IMDG and IATA. Responsible for substance registration under TSCA & CEPA including Low Volume and Polymer Exemptions. Manage product stewardship, compliance and process improvement projects. Report obligations for TSCA Chemical Data Reporting and CEPA Section 71 Surveys. Provide business with regulatory insight, troubleshooting, and training. Responsible for process documentation and creating/implementing SOPs. If you have... Bachelor’s degree in Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, Environmental Science, or a related field of study (or foreign equivalent) and 5 years of related experience required. Experience must include three (3) years of: product regulations, regulatory affairs, product stewardship, or related fields in EHS; Engage with global and regional business contacts, working within a cross-functional, multi-BU, virtual and matrixed business environment; SAP EHS module, Global Trade Control System, and Global Label Management and SDS creation; Data extraction from SAP; data analysis; classification and labeling of mixtures according to the Globally Harmonized System (GHS); Creation of Safety Data Sheets and product labels based on country specific requirements; Dangerous Goods regulations; regulations affecting BASF products, markets, and system; BASF Chemetall product families and the chemistry associated with them; and managing projects. 5-10% domestic and international travel. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact Careers.NorthAmerica@basf.com to report fraud. Pay transparency BASF is committed to pay transparency practices. The competitive Pay Range for this role is $116,147-$132,975. Actual pay will be determined based on education, certifications, experience, and other job-related factors permitted by law. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Come create chemistry with us! BASF’s Catalysts division is the global market leader in catalyst technologies. The division develops and produces mobile emissions catalysts as well as process catalysts and offers precious metals trading, recycling and related products and services. During your shift as a 1st Class Mechanic, you will Recognize and practice safe work habits in all daily duties. Install, rearrange, align, dismantle, repair, and reassemble complex equipment; diagnose complex equipment failures. Perform mechanic‑skill work including fabrication, assembly, welding, burning, cutting, and pipefitting using oxyacetylene, plasma arc, shielded arc, tungsten arc, flux‑cored, carbon arc cutting and gouging, and submerged arc methods. Occasionally perform work in other trades (pipefitter, rigger, rough carpentry, machinist, etc.). Use typical mechanic’s tools, tool‑room inspection equipment, inspection gages, and involved shop math; operate production equipment to check work. Work from drawings, sketches, or verbal instructions and read, comprehend, and execute tasks from technical operating and maintenance manuals. Inspect own and others’ work for quality and dimensional requirements. Clean debris and lubricant from parts, equipment, and the work area. Repair piping and valves; install new pipe, valves, and control devices for equipment modifications. Rig equipment for lifting with hoists or cranes, routinely handling loads of several thousand pounds. Complete routine paperwork and use basic computer skills to access CMMS information. If you... Hold a High School diploma or equivalent. Have prior experience working with rotating equipment or equivalent in a manufacturing environment. Are able to start, stop and operate all shop equipment. Can work with water soluble and oil-based lubricants. Are able to work on ladders, scaffolds and in pits and manually lift 50# intermittently (Heavier parts are lifted using other methods). Electrical Engineer Staff Houston, TX (Hybrid) We are looking for a Electrical Engineer Staff to join our Global Engineering team in Houston, TX Come create chemistry with us! At BASF, we create chemistry through the power of connected minds. As the world’s leading chemical company, we help our customers in nearly every industry meet the current and future needs of society through science and innovation. The Global Engineering team leverages our technical know-how and expertise to deliver solutions that enable safe, sustainable, reliable, and cost-competitive production. As a Staff Electrical Engineer Lead within the Electrical and Instrument Design Expertise team, you will oversee electrical design activities across all phases of greenfield and brownfield capital projects for BASF Corporation. In this role, you will be responsible for developing project scopes, preparing cost estimates, specifying and procuring electrical equipment, producing construction work packages, ensuring design quality, and providing technical support through startup and commissioning. You will collaborate closely with internal stakeholders and external engineering partners to ensure successful project execution that aligns with BASF’s technical standards and business objectives. A critical component of this position is the ability to develop, execute, and interpret electrical system studies, delivering clear, accurate, and detailed reports that support safe, reliable, and compliant system designs. As a Electrical Engineer Staff, you create chemistry by... Providing technical leadership for the execution of capital projects in chemical and petrochemical environments. Leading development of electrical scope, engineering deliverables, and specifications, including manhour estimates, material take‑offs, power system studies, and equipment selection. Executing and reviewing electrical system studies, ensuring NEC compliance, reliability, and remediation across BASF sites. Preparing cost estimates, performing technical bid evaluations, and supporting procurement activities to secure compliant and cost‑effective electrical equipment. Providing technical oversight during installation, testing, energization, startup, and commissioning, while supporting maintenance activities and troubleshooting construction‑phase challenges. Collaborating with multidisciplinary teams, including operations, maintenance, procurement, project engineering, vendors, and external engineering partners to drive aligned and efficient project execution. Driving innovative, safe, and sustainable engineering solutions that enhance reliability and improve efficiency and predictability within the capital project design process. Communicating effectively across technical and project teams, contributing to project meetings, continuous improvement initiatives, and value‑based engineering discussions. If you... Hold a BS/MS degree in Electrical Engineering. Have a minimum of 5 years’ experience in electrical engineering in the Chemicals, Refining or Petrochemicals industry. Have Capital Project execution experience, especially in the Electrical discipline areas. Are proficient with SKM Power Tools for Windows. Have strong interpersonal, communication, and networking skills with ability to lead business development activities along with cross-functional teams and lead workshops. Possess knowledge of electrical expertise (equipment and infrastructure) within industry (chemical preferred). Have prior experience working with Engineering Partners and teams with diverse cultural backgrounds Possess experience in leading efforts to identify and implement electrical improvements in the Chemicals, Refining or Petrochemicals industry. Have strong Customer Focus skills and Entrepreneurial mind set. At BASF, we create chemistry through the power of connected minds. By balancing economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility, we are building a more sustainable future through chemistry. As the world’s leading chemical company, we help our customers in nearly every industry meet the current and future needs of society through science and innovation. We provide a challenging and rewarding work environment with a strong emphasis on process safety, as well as the safety of our employees and the communities in which we operate. The maintenance supervisor has responsibility for the maintenance department at the Greenville, Ohio site.  This role ensures maintenance work is completed safely, correctly, and timely to support 24/7 production assets. The supervisor oversees about 8 hourly maintenance and I&E personnel. This role reports to the maintenance & engineering manager. Maintenance is critical to the site meeting business and financial obligations.  In addition, the maintenance supervisor is part of the extended leadership team, along with supervisors from the other site departments. What you'll do: Lead site maintenance, including daily execution and participation in longer-term strategies. Execute maintenance goals and objectives, monitor performance against those objectives, hold the team accountable to the goals and KPI’s, and coach/manage personnel performance accordingly. Identify and execute improvement opportunities in maintenance workflow and execution to improve safety, cost, and efficiency. Reduce waste and drive continuous improvement in the maintenance department. Provide troubleshooting support when needed by crafts or operations. Exhibit good decision-making that supports business needs and maintains a safe culture. Develop and cultivate good working relationships with supervisors in other departments. Ensure a customer-focused approach to supporting a 24/7 production site. If you... Starting pay $18.00/hr. and progressing to $25.00/hr. At BASF, we are proud to introduce the next generation of Process Operations Technician to the innovative world of Manufacturing. If you are looking to build a meaningful career while making an impact on the world, then come create chemistry with us! Come create chemistry with us! As a Process Operations Technician Apprentice in our Chattanooga, TN, polymer dispersions plant you will make an impact on the world with a fundamental focus on sustainability. As a Process Operations Technician Apprentice, you will be a part of a team which manufacturers products used formulations for construction chemicals, adhesives, printing/packaging products and more! description:

Account Manager Houston, TX (Remote) Company vehicle offered We are looking for an Account Manager to join our Performance Chemicals team in Houston, TX. Come create chemistry with us! As an innovative partner, BASF’s Performance Chemicals division offers chemicals for various customer industries, such as plastics, automotive, refining, lubricants, oilfield and mining. Our highly qualified and experienced team with outstanding market knowledge as well as our innovation platform and application know-how ensure our technological competence to provide excellent solutions to our customers. As an Key Account Manager – Refinery Catalysts, you will serve as the primary commercial and technical interface between BASF and refinery customers. You will be responsible for driving profitable growth, strengthening strategic partnerships, and delivering measurable value through catalyst solutions and services. This role blends technical expertise, commercial leadership, and relationship management to support refinery performance, reliability, and profitability As a Account Manager, you create chemistry by... Acting as the face of BASF to assigned refinery clients, building multi-level relationships across operations, technical, procurement, and leadership teams, while developing and executing account strategies focused on growth, retention, and value creation. Achieving annual sales targets, margin goals, and market share objectives through contract negotiations, pricing strategy, commercial agreements, accurate sales forecasting, pipeline management, and CRM maintenance. Applying knowledge of FCC, hydroprocessing, and refining catalyst technologies to support customer operations through catalyst performance reviews, economic value assessments, technical presentations, and recommendations that translate technical results into economic and operational benefits. Overseeing implementation and performance of catalyst programs, including trials, new product introductions, performance tracking and reporting, troubleshooting, optimization, and coordination of internal and external stakeholders to ensure high-quality execution. Leading regular business reviews with customers to review KPIs, performance, and value delivered; align on customer priorities and operational goals; identify risks and growth opportunities; and develop joint continuous improvement plans that strengthen long-term partnerships. Working closely with BASF teams including Technical Service/Engineering, Supply Chain, and Marketing & Product Management to coordinate resources and deliver integrated customer-focused solutions. Monitoring refinery market trends, customer strategies, and competitive activity to provide insights that support pricing, product positioning, and growth strategy. If you... Hold a Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, with Chemical Engineering preferred, or a related field. Bring a minimum of 5 years of experience in chemical engineering, preferably within the refinery or chemical industry, with a proven track record managing large accounts and driving business growth. Preferred: Have experience within catalysts or related industries to support customer engagement and technical understanding, as well as 3+ years in account management or sales. Demonstrate a strong technical understanding of refinery processes. Possess excellent communication and interpersonal skills to build relationships with clients and internal teams. Have strong analytical, negotiation, problem-solving, and organizational skills, including the ability to analyze market trends and customer needs, develop strategic sales plans, and manage multiple accounts simultaneously. Are proficient with MS Office, CRM software, and other sales management tools. Are willing to travel up to 50% for customer meetings and industry events as needed. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. At BASF/Chemetall , we develop and manufacture tailor-made technology and system solutions for applied surface technology. Our products protect metals from corrosion, facilitate forming and treatment, prepare parts optimally for the painting process and ensure excellent coating adhesion. Our technologies and products are used in a variety of industries and end markets such as automotive, aerospace, aluminum finishing and metal forming. The chemical treatment of metal surfaces is BASF/Chemetall core competence. The focus of our worldwide activities is on the development and implementation of customized technology and system solutions for surface treatment. Where the Chemistry Happens: The District Manager oversees sales operations within a specific geographic area, leading a team to achieve sales targets and drive revenue growth. Formula for Success Key Responsibilities Executing strategic sales plans to meet district quotas and expand market share within their assigned territory. Team Leadership: They lead and manage a team of sales representatives, providing coaching, training, and performance feedback to enhance their skills and effectiveness. Performance Analysis: Analysing sales data and market trends is crucial for identifying new business opportunities and adjusting sales strategies accordingly. Field Visits: Regular field visits with sales representatives allow them to observe techniques, offer direct coaching, and support client interactions. Collaboration: They work closely with Key Industry Sales and Marketing to ensure alignment of sales efforts with company goals and provide market feedback. People Management Assign Sales and Profit targets to align to strategic initiatives Performance management, talent development and talent retention Develop and maintain succession plan for the Sale’s District Hire, train and coach Technical Sales Representative, to develop effective Sales techniques, Technical problem solving, product knowledge and awareness of customer needs Hire and identified needed training to develop effective Technical problem solving, product knowledge and awareness of customer needs Customer Development Manage pipeline and target accounts to meet our growth objectives. Execute Strategy of targets as identified as part of District Strategic Roadmap in collaboration with Regional Marketing and Regional Sales- Industries Execution of global strategies within each Industry Segment key customers Keep track of competitors’ offers and action building a competitive awareness Provide input for the development of pricing strategies Account and Relationship Management Responsible for Business reviews completion at top 20 accounts Develop strong business relationships with key decision-makers Manage sales and pricing processes to align with the company’s pricing guidelines and marketing pricing strategy Coordinate and execute sales strategies to develop target business Prepare Supply Agreements within the given authorization framework Other Accountabilities and Admin Internal Lead the effort and education for the digital processes (Salesforce, Power BI, Power Apps etc) Coordinate with Industry Sales personnel on target account activities Share market insights and trends with Sales Managers, Key Industry Sales management and Regional Marketing management Manage AR and Consignment Inventory to assigned goals. Ingredients for Success BS Degree Desired 3-5 years of sales experience and leadership Industrial Technical Sales experience including pretreatment, surface treatment, and metalworking in the Specialty Chemical Industry. Sales and Business acumen (account & relationship management, value-based selling, etc.) Leadership skills: ability to motivate and develop a sales team Excellent communication and presentation skills Strong cross-functional collaboration skills Strategic thinking skills Travel The District Manager will reside in his / her assigned District geography. This individual will provide complete sales and service which ensures safe quality product performance while maximizing profitability within their assigned territory. Formula for Success: Sales Analysis and Planning Understands the business activities of accounts in the territory and applies knowledge of the customer’s internal operation to tailor sales presentations and sales proposals to the account’s specific needs. Manages territory profitability by reviewing territory compensation margins, providing margin improvement by product selection and optimum pricing.  Assures that technology provided within the territory strengthens the company’s competitive position within the accounts. Maintains contacts with customers in relation to their service requirements. Provides information on new products, methods, techniques and other general information such as safety and disposal. Trains customers and insures proper and safe use of Chemetall materials. Reports competitive activities, that impact sales in the territory, to the supervisor (District Manager) Prepares and submits call reports, exception reports, and in-plant service reports via CRM (Salesforce) Use of the CRM ( Salesforce) for management of the accounts. Share reports with appropriate with colleagues to ensure collaboration with different functions to keep all necessary information on all accounts Sales Support Activities Improves technical knowledge of Chemetall materials through self-study and participation in professional Association. Participates in team selling activities in cooperation with other TSR’s, technical support personnel and application engineers. Requests samples according to company policy. Exercises proper caution in handling company materials and equipment. Operates automobile (company car) safely and maintains it in proper operating condition. Complete all necessary reports in due time ( expenses, mileage report, etc.) Ingredients for Success: BS Degree in Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, Engineering, or Business is required. Minimum of 2 years of relatable experience is required. B2B/outside industrial sales experience, in specialty chemical sales (surface treatment-paint pretreatments, wastewater treatments, metalworking fluids, etc.) preferred. description:

This individual will provide complete sales and service which ensures safe quality product performance while maximizing profitability within their assigned territory. This position offers a company car, laptop, phone, and all travel expenses covered by the company; travel can be anywhere from 50-60%. Formula for Success: Sales Analysis and Planning Understands the business activities of accounts in the territory and applies knowledge of the customer’s internal operation to tailor sales presentations and sales proposals to the account’s specific needs. Manages territory profitability by reviewing territory compensation margins, providing margin improvement by product selection and optimum pricing.  Assures that technology provided within the territory strengthens the company’s competitive position within the accounts. Plans a weekly itinerary of calls to allocate time effectively and to ensure proper territory coverage by industry, geography and account potential. Sales Execution Calls on established and prospective customers, determines their needs, and sells appropriate products to markets, targeted by the business units of the company. Makes proper number of calls per day based on territory requirements. Must be able to physically service and call on existing and prospective customers. Selects the process and the right material to solve customer cleaning, sanitation or surface treatment problems. Makes sales presentations, both orally and in written. Answers customers questions concerning product use. Price negotiation: Provide pricing quotes, price and delivery policy within established limits, subject to approval. Attain sales plan by securing new customers, increasing sales to current customers and maintaining up to date opportunity pipeline in CRM (Salesforces) Customer Service Works closely with customers focusing on building strong business relationships. Maintains contacts with customers in relation to their service requirements. Provides information on new products, methods, techniques and other general information such as safety and disposal. Trains customers and insures proper and safe use of Chemetall materials. Reports competitive activities, that impact sales in the territory, to the supervisor (District Manager) Prepares and submits call reports, exception reports, and in-plant service reports via CRM (Salesforce) Use of the CRM ( Salesforce) for management of the accounts. Share reports with appropriate with colleagues to ensure collaboration with different functions to keep all necessary information on all accounts . Sales Support Activities Improves technical knowledge of Chemetall materials through self-study and participation in professional association. Participates in team selling activities in cooperation with other TSR’s, technical support personnel and application engineers. Requests samples according to company policy. Exercises proper caution in handling company materials and equipment. Operates automobile (company car) safely and maintains it in proper operating condition. Complete all necessary reports in due time ( expenses, mileage report, etc.) Ingredients for Success: BS Degree in Chemistry, Chemical Engineering, Engineering, or Business is required. Minimum of 2 years of relatable experience is required. B2B/outside industrial sales experience, in specialty chemical sales (surface treatment-paint pretreatments, wastewater treatments, metalworking fluids, etc.) preferred. Ability to make sales presentations and follow up with written proposals and manage customer contracts. Well organized with sales planning. Managing customer accounts within the assigned territory. Strong selling skills for customer acquisition and continuous up-/cross-selling opportunities (customer support/account management) Build and strengthen customer relationships. Technical consulting capabilities regarding products and processes; will learn and understand Chemetall product portfolio. Strong business acumen. Strong Negotiation skills Problem solving skills Time management Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal. Travel up to 50-60%, with occasional overnight travel. Valid driver's license in good standing. Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. Our total offer includes a

description:

Industry Sales Specialist - Metalworking Fluids (ECT-Chemetall) Come create chemistry with us! BASF/Chemetall, is the Surface Treatment global business unit of BASF’s Coatings division, operating under the Chemetall brand, and a leading supplier of applied surface treatments worldwide. At BASF/Chemetall, we develop and manufacture tailor-made technology and system solutions for applied surface technology. Our products protect metals from corrosion, facilitate forming and treatment, prepare parts optimally for the painting process and ensure excellent coating adhesion. Our technologies and products are used in a variety of industries and end markets such as automotive, aerospace, aluminum finishing and metal forming. The chemical treatment of metal surfaces is BASF/Chemetall core competence. The focus of our worldwide activities is on the development and implementation of customized technology and system solutions for surface treatment. We are looking for a Industry Sales Specialist-Metalworking Fluids to join our Sales team; this is a remote position that covers North America.  This individual can be based in the U.S., Canada, or Mexico; however, near an international airport. The Industry Sales Specialist-Metal Working Fluids (MWF) will cover N.A. and be responsible for growing sales by proving the whole array of MWF products as systematic up/cross-selling. As a Industry Sales Specialist - Metalworking Fluids (ECT-Chemetall), you create chemistry by... Customer Development & Sales Leadership •    Lead customer development strategy in collaboration with the Key Industry Sales Manager, including account segmentation, target setting, and multi-site growth planning. •    Own and support sales forecasting and pipeline accuracy by partnering with local sales teams and actively managing opportunities in Salesforce. •    Proactively identify, qualify, and advance up-sell and cross-sell opportunities across complex, multi-location customer organizations using data-driven account insights. •    Prospect and introduce new products and solutions •    Monitor competitive activity, pricing, and solution positioning; develop and execute competitive response strategies. Account & Relationship Management Objectives •    Support the negotiation, structuring, and closing of complex sales agreements with strategic and multi-site accounts, •    Own and coordinate commercial and technical approval processes, ensuring timely decision-making and seamless execution across internal stakeholders. •    Act as the primary strategic liaison between assigned accounts and all Chemetall functions, •    Develop and sustain executive-level relationships with key customer decision-makers, influencers, and site leaders •    Identify and support key customer business reviews to assess performance and  align on strategic priorities. Other Accountabilities and Administrative •    Advise regional sales team on account(s) activities. •    Share market insights and trends with Regional Marketing management. •    Provides input into segment strategy. If you have... Bachelor’s degree in chemical engineering, chemistry, engineering, or business administration is preferred. Experience in Metal Working Fluids is required. High level of efficiency in sales process. Project management skills. Demonstrated business acumen. Sales acumen (account & relationship management, value-based selling, etc.) Customer focus Organizational awareness Strong written and oral communication skills. Strong collaboration mindset. Flexibility and ability to travel up to 50%, 25% overnight travel. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF Adding value to our customers begins with adding value to you. you@BASF is the suite of benefits, perks, programs and unique opportunities we offer to support you – the whole you – in all stages of your life and career. With you@BASF, you create your own chemistry. The total rewards that you receive as a BASF employee go way beyond a paycheck. From competitive health and insurance plans to robust retirement benefits that include company-matching contributions, to making sure you never stop learning, we believe investing in you is investing in our success. Working for a large, global organization, you’ll have a chance to grow professionally and personally, expand your network and build a rewarding and dynamic career.  We also provide engaging learning and development opportunities to help you make the most of your talents and your role. About us As the world’s leading chemical company, we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! We constantly strive to become an even better workplace. As a result of our commitment to our employees, BASF has earned various awards, including being recognized by Mediacorp in 2025 as one of Canada's Top Employers for eleventh consecutive year, one of the Greater Toronto Area's Top Employers for the twelfth year in a row, and twice being named one of Canada's Top Employers for Young People and Best Diversity Employers. We provide a fulfilling work environment with a strong emphasis on the physical and psychological safety of our employees and the communities we operate in. We are always working to form the best team with an emphasis on diversity, equity, and inclusion and through lifelong learning and development. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy Statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request personal or financial information of any kind via either text message or direct message on any social media platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never offer a candidate a position and request a cheque for equipment or any other type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact myhr@basf.com to report fraud. Pay Transparency BASF is committed to pay transparency practices. The competitive Pay Range for this role is $120,000-$160,000 CAD. Actual pay will be determined based on education, certifications, experience, and other job-related factors permitted by law. Inclusion and Equal Opportunity Employment BASF is dedicated to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace where our employees can be their authentic selves. We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, place of origin, ethnic origin, colour, religion/creed, sex/pregnancy, marital status, family status, disability status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by law. We seek to make our recruitment process accessible for applicants with disabilities. If you require accommodations or support throughout the recruitment process, please contact Careers.NorthAmerica@basf.com with the subject line “Recruitment Accommodation Request”. description:

Senior Scientist Advance Clean and Etch Hillsboro, OR (Onsite) Come create chemistry with us! We are looking for a Wet Cleans R & D Application Engineer to join our cutting R & D team focused on developing new products in the semiconductor manufacturing space.  The position will be based in Hillsboro, Oregon, supporting the overall success of the Electronic Materials Global Business Unit (GBU) of BASF. At BASF Electronic Materials, we uphold a relentless passion to innovate the future of electronics with our customers by equipping them with the essential solutions and technologies to continuously succeed in the competitive electronics industry. BASF has been supplying electronic materials for more than 30 years. The experience we have earned and the chemical expertise we possess enables us to offer outstanding products and solutions to our customers. At BASF, we offer high purity and quality process chemicals and specialized solutions for semiconductors, flat panel displays, lighting such as LED as well as photovoltaics. With our team of passionate, dedicated experts from all facets of the industry fields working closely with our customers worldwide, we are steadfast in our philosophy to deliver the quality excellence, reliable services, and innovative solutions that exceed the highest standards. The Senior Scientist will be responsible for carrying out R & D activities the business area of Advanced Cleaning and Etching in North America. The ideal candidates will bring extensive expertise in the wet cleans applications with a passion of innovation and strong leadership skills. As a Senior Scientist Advance Clean and Etch, you create chemistry by... Drive innovation efforts to develop chemical formulations for precise cleaning and etching to fulfill customer requirements. Design and execute experiments to evaluate the performance of BASF commercial and R&D samples in customer applications. Participate in customer projects and meetings as a member of the global product development team. Communicate experimental results, observed trends and aggregated experience with customers, internal partners, and management on a regular basis. Work closely with technical marketing team to discover unmet needs and help turn ideas into new product development projects/topics. Work within multidisciplinary project teams which include EH&S, Engineering, Procurement and Logistics to provide contributions to the Hillsboro site operation. Implement EH&S rule based on BASF guideline. Accept personal responsibility to act according to process safety regulations and company directives. Promotes the quality systems by monitoring, reviewing, and implementing detailed and accurate documentation processes for all product development and analytical activities and ensure the proper execution of SOPs in wet cleans domain. If you have... PhD degree in Chemistry, Chemical Engineering or Materials Science and Engineering 3+ year research/product development experience in chemistry, chemical engineering or semiconductor manufacturing related field. 2+ years of research experience in colloidal chemistry and dispersions and/or surface chemistry Effective communication skills to facilitate discussions with internal personnel and customers. Ability to effectively communicate detailed and precise technical requirement, procedures, and results in oral and written format. Management experience in directing, coordinating, and managing team activities to accomplish highly technical tasks. Experienced in lab operations. Able to operate in environment of frequently changing priorities. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. name: Pasadena Site Director
description:
Pasadena Site Director Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description Pasadena Site Director LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now Now hiring! Pasadena Site Director Pasadena, TX (Onsite) Relocation assistance available We are looking for a Pasadena Site Director to join our Petrochemicals team in Pasadena, TX. Where the chemistry happens… BASF’s Petrochemical division is the starting point of BASF's petrochemical value creation worldwide, also known as “the heart of the Verbund,” representing the world's largest chemical markets. Together, let us keep the heart beating one creative solution at a time! As the Site Director for the Pasadena site, you will lead a highly regulated and complex chemical manufacturing operation with full accountability for safety, compliance, and business performance. You will be responsible for a significant site operating budget, with full accountability for cost performance and financial discipline. You will lead and develop a team of approximately 60 employees, including shift personnel and direct reports across EHS, production, technology, maintenance, asset management, laboratory, quality, and site services. Come create chemistry with us! As a Pasadena Site Director, you create chemistry by... Owning the site’s license to operate by ensuring full compliance with all environmental permits, regulatory requirements, and Responsible Care standards, recognizing that compliance failures can directly impact production continuity. Driving a strong EHS culture and operational risk management, with focus on critical systems such as flare reliability, emergency response readiness, and incident prevention. Leading and developing a high-performing leadership team and broader organization of ~60 employees, fostering ownership, accountability, and continuous improvement. Establishing strong leadership cadences and governance systems, ensuring KPI ownership, performance transparency, and disciplined execution across all site functions. Representing BASF externally as the accountable site leader, managing relationships with regulators, community stakeholders, and key partners (e.g., site infrastructure providers), ensuring trust and compliance. Leading and governing process safety and compliance systems, including RC audits, PSM/RMP programs, regulatory reporting, and permit renewals, ensuring disciplined execution and continuous audit readiness. Managing a large-scale manufacturing operation with full accountability for site performance, including operating costs and capital investments. Overseeing and prioritizing an annual CapEx investment portfolio to support reliability, compliance, and long-term competitiveness. If you... Have a bachelor’s degree in engineering, chemistry, or a related technical field, along with significant experience in chemical manufacturing or other high-hazard industrial environments. Bring deep expertise in process safety, environmental compliance, and regulatory frameworks, with proven accountability for maintaining a site’s license to operate. Demonstrate strong business acumen, with experience managing large and complex operating budgets and driving cost efficiency and asset performance. Have a track record of leading organizations of comparable size and complexity, successfully managing multidisciplinary teams and developing leaders. Are confident engaging with a broad range of stakeholders, including regulators, community representatives, and external partners. Bring a results-driven mindset with the ability to lead transformation, strengthen competitiveness, and sustain long-term operational excellence in complex environments. Customer Site Service Manager Customer Site Service Manager (CSSM) Ford Michigan Assembly Plant Location: Wayne, MI Come create chemistry with us! Business Unit EC is driving surface solutions! We are a global expert in the development, production, and marketing of coatings and solutions. We are looking for a professional like you to join our team! This position supports the daily operation of BASF products in Customer Automotive Assembly Plants by providing on-site technical and application knowledge. The role responds to customer requirements, enforces product quality/performance specification, and supports new product/application development. The Customer Site Service Manager (CSSM) will provide on-site expertise of product performance including color management, defect identification, and corrective action if necessary. S/he will develop and support customer relationships. This role supports the efforts of a BASF sales/technical team to assure value to the customer for product technical support, problem investigation, analysis and resolution. The position assures product performance meets or exceeds customer expectations and supports business objectives to be our customer’s supplier of choice. The incumbent will demonstrate technical and professional expertise to enhance BASF’s reputation with the customer. S/he will integrate within the business and technical community to bring additional value to ECO coatings customers. The role controls key performance indicators within product service environments to drive problem resolution and product improvement initiatives critical to customer performance expectations. As a Customer Site Service Manager, you create chemistry by... Supporting the daily operation of BASF products in Customer Automotive Assembly Plants. Providing on-site technical and application expertise of product performance including color management, defect identification, and corrective action if necessary. Assuring product performance meets or exceeds customer expectations and supports business objectives to be our customer’s supplier of choice. Responding to customer requirements, enforcing product quality/performance specification, and supporting new product/application development. Supports the efforts of a BASF sales/technical team to assure value to the customer for product technical support, problem investigation, analysis, and resolution. Managing existing relationships and developing new ones. If you have... Bachelor’s Degree or High School Diploma (or GED) plus Equivalent Experience 6+ years of Automotive Coatings Industry experience preferred. Leveraging your education and extensive background in paint applications and manufacturing you will provide on-site expertise of products, their application and performance. Your superior communication abilities will allow you to influence all levels of the customer organization, implement action plans and develop and achieve customer metrics. Your strong entrepreneurial drive and business acumen will allow you to identify improvement opportunities, develop and execute a plan to achieve them in a cost-effective and timely manner as well as manage future plant production and product launches. Laboratory Technical Specialist Houston, TX or Cincinnati, OH (On Site) Relocation assistance is offered for this position. We are looking for a Laboratory Technical Specialist to join our Performance Chemicals team. Come create chemistry with us! As an innovative partner, BASF’s Performance Chemicals division offers chemicals for various customer industries, such as plastics, automotive, refining, lubricants, oilfield and mining. Our highly qualified and experienced team with outstanding market knowledge as well as our innovation platform and application know-how ensure our technological competence to provide excellent solutions to our customers. We are seeking a professional like you to perform a variety of lubricant/fuel additive evaluation tests using both established and custom procedures. You will collaborate with Marketing, Sales, and Product Management of Fuel and Lubricant Solutions to complete test plans for technical requests. As a Laboratory Technical Specialist, you create chemistry by... Ensuring proper communication of experimental findings, typically as written reports. Performing a preliminary assessment of the technical information, identifying when results are unusual or suspect, and investigating the cause of suspect data. Defining and implementing corrective action. Possessing mechanical aptitude to maintain, troubleshoot, and provide solutions for the operation of a wide variety of bench test equipment and peripheral lab utilities. Possessing an understanding of Power BI and other digitalization tools to assist in improving our lab efficiency. Implementing the Quality initiatives of the laboratory. Developing contacts with colleagues in other departments to perform job responsibilities. If you... Have a B.S., M.S., or Ph.D. in Chemistry or a related scientific discipline, with 5+ years of experience for a B.S., 2–3 years for an M.S., or minimal experience for a Ph.D., in laboratory testing—preferably in the lubricant or fuel testing area. Hold strong mechanical aptitude and analytical thinking abilities. Possess a high level of efficiency and good time management skills, with the ability to multi-task independently. Have strong attention to detail, quality orientation, and are concise and detailed in report writing and excellent in record-keeping. Are a self-starter with the ability to perform functions with limited supervision. Have solid communication skills and are adept at using the Microsoft suite of applications, including Power BI, and familiarity with LIMS system. Are able to travel intermittently to Tarrytown, NY on a monthly basis through the remainder of 2026. Learning and Development Coordinator Freeport, TX We are looking for a Learning and Development Coordinator to join our Petrochemicals team in Freeport, TX. Come create chemistry with us! The Petrochemicals division is the starting point of BASF’s petrochemical-based value chains throughout the regions. The division manufactures and markets a broad portfolio of high-quality basic chemicals and tailored specialties for internal and external customers. As a Learning and Development Coordinator supporting the Acrylates plants at Freeport, you will be responsible for facilitating the development, implementation, and documentation of training curriculum for the Acrylates production and maintenance team, including specific process training, technical skills, refresher training, contractor orientation, and/or new employee qualification training as required. This job is responsible for continuous improvement in the areas of employee training and development, ensuring that job-specific and technical training is effective, consistent and sustainable. During your shift as a Learning and Development Coordinator, you will Utilize your experience with employee coaching, instruction, in-the-field training, and classroom-style presentations to deliver effective training and development programs. Facilitate the development, implementation, and documentation of unit employee training and testing using Document Control Databases and Computer-Based Training programs to ensure compliance with Unit, Site, Corporate, and regulatory requirements. Develop and deliver one-on-one instruction, in-the-field training, and classroom presentations, while leveraging your knowledge of chemical process operations to support training requirements and equipment-specific learning initiatives. Assess training needs, translate job requirements into training plans, and prepare and organize new procedures and training requirements into instructional materials using strong technical communication skills. If you... Hold an Associate’s degree and have three (3) years of manufacturing experience in a plant environment, or a High School Diploma/GED with a minimum of seven (7) years of manufacturing experience. Have a minimum of four (4) years of experience using Microsoft Office applications. Preferred: Bring a minimum of five (5) years of experience with operating procedures and equipment used in chemical manufacturing operations. Preferred: Have a minimum of five (5) years of experience with DCS-based control systems and associated training requirements. Preferred: Are experienced in managing or developing training in an LMS. Preferred: Have experience administering training and hands on training. Preferred: Possess experience with PSM/RMP audits as well as PSM/RMP covered process. description:

Quality Assurance Technician Wyandotte, MI (Onsite) Night Shift Differential. Come create chemistry with us! The Performance Materials division is at the forefront of the much-needed sustainability transformation in plastics. Our experts co-create products with customers to bring innovations to major industry sectors such as transportation, consumer goods, industrial applications, and construction. These solutions contribute to a sustainable future by pushing the boundaries in thermal resistance, robustness and lightweight applications. We are seeking a professional like you to join our team as a Quality Assurance Technician in the Wyandotte Polyol plant – makers of high-performance conventional polyols and graft polyols used in automotive, bedding, filtration, and other specialty applications. In this role, you will work rotating 12-hour shifts and be responsible for quality assurance testing of raw materials, intermediates, and final products. You will apply your basic understanding of general chemistry and ISO 9001 requirements to maintain quality control as a key member of the manufacturing team. * “This position is part of a job family with multiple levels and will be filled at a level that is commensurate with the candidate's qualifications and experience.” During your 12-hour rotating shift as a Quality Assurance Technician, you will Accurately performing analysis of raw materials, intermediates, and finished goods at our polyol plant according to written procedures and delivering the results to the production team in a timely manner to ensure smooth plant operations. Gain proficiency in utilizing SAP database systems, accurately inputting all test data into SAP, and organizing quality records accordingly. Understand ISO 9001 quality standards and supporting the laboratory in compliance with regulations and standards. Engage within the organization and frequently interacting with colleagues, the production team, and the management team to deliver results, and discuss any quality related problems, and provide innovative ideas to improve lab and plant processes. Support the laboratory in ensuring suitability of laboratory equipment through proper operation, maintenance, calibration, verification, and good quality control and assessment activities. Order lab supplies in a timely manner, maintaining the inventory of laboratory chemicals, storing and labeling chemicals appropriately while adhering to OSHA laboratory. If you... • Have a minimum of an Associate's degree or college diploma with 2 years of experience working in a laboratory setting, or a B.S. in Chemistry or related field. • Are willing and able to work rotating 12-hour shifts, including nights, weekends, and holidays when necessary. • Have flexibility with scheduling to support the team during opening shifts. • Possess familiarity and experience with specific laboratory equipment such as Karl Fischer titrator and Gas Chromatograph (considered as an additional qualification). description:

Account Manager - Human Nutrition, Vitamins & Carotenoids Remote US - West Coast preferred We are looking for an Account Manager - Human Nutrition, Vitamins & Carotenoids to join our Nutrition Ingredients team and manage customers across the US. Come create chemistry with us! BASF Nutrition & Health (EN) offers a comprehensive range of products and services for the human and animal nutrition, pharmaceutical and fragrance & flavor industries. Our products meet the highest safety, regulatory and sustainability standards. Together with our customers, we actively contribute to improving the nutrition and health of consumers around the world and to driving sustainable development. In this role, you will be responsible for managing and strengthening strategic relationships with key customers in the Human Nutrition business, providing ingredients for dietary supplements, food and beverages, functional foods and other health and nutrition applications. This includes driving account strategies, supporting innovation projects, and expanding BASF’s position as a trusted partner for high-quality ingredients that help improve nutrition, wellness, and everyday health. As an Account Manager - Human Nutrition, Vitamins & Carotenoids, you create chemistry by... Leading customer development and growth activities in alignment with the Human Nutrition strategy across Sales, Marketing, and Account Management. Driving the key account planning process, including developing and executing account plans for customers in the USA. Identifying, negotiating, and closing growth opportunities with customers while managing key internal and external stakeholders. Implementing the Human Nutrition strategy and delivering performance targets by defining budgets, monitoring critical KPIs, and driving new business and innovation initiatives. Acting as the primary customer focal point, serving as the key interface for BASF senior management and leading customer issue escalation and resolution. Building and maintaining senior-level customer relationships by presenting to internal and external stakeholders and steering high-impact business discussions. If you... Hold a Bachelor’s or advanced degree and bring 5+ years of business experience in Sales, Marketing, or Account Management. Demonstrate proven sales leadership and influencing skills, with experience supporting strategic account planning. Apply strong strategic thinking and organizational awareness to manage complex stakeholder environments and drive mutually beneficial outcomes. Work collaboratively with an entrepreneurial and solution-oriented mindset across functions. Leverage strong business acumen, including experience managing budgets, KPIs, and business performance targets. Communicate effectively at a senior level, possessing strong interpersonal, presentation, and Microsoft Office skills to engage key decision-makers. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request financial information of any kind via email, private text message or direct message on any social medial platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never send a candidate a check for equipment or request any type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact Careers.NorthAmerica@basf.com to report fraud. Pay transparency BASF is committed to pay transparency practices. The competitive pay range for this role is $128,000 – $160,000. Actual pay will be determined based on education, certifications, experience, and other job-related factors permitted by law. Equal employment opportunities We are an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, color, religion, sex, marital status, national origin, disability status, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis. Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. Logistics Specialist McIntosh, AL (Onsite) Relocation assistance is offered We are looking for a Logistics Specialist to join our Performance Chemicals team in McIntosh. Come create chemistry with us! As an innovative partner, BASF’s Performance Chemicals division offers chemicals for various customer industries, such as plastics, automotive, refining, lubricants, oilfield and mining. Our highly qualified and experienced team with outstanding market knowledge as well as our innovation platform and application know-how ensure our technological competence to provide excellent solutions to our customers. As a Logistics Specialist you will be responsible for the safe, compliant, and cost-effective execution of all site logistics activities, including domestic and international shipping and receiving. This role coordinates with carriers and warehouse resources, manages orders and deliveries, and ensures customer and regulatory requirements all while maintaining/executing all associated SAP transactions and shipping documents. As a Logistics Specialist, you create chemistry by... Coordinating inbound and outbound logistics for packaged and bulk materials, including shipping and receiving, verification, storage, and scheduling. Overseeing warehouse and yard operations, including space management, staging, trailer availability, and trailer inventory management. Maintaining inventory accuracy through regular cycle counts, investigation of discrepancies, and implementation of corrective actions, while ensuring accurate SAP inventory transactions. Investigating customer complaints and determining appropriate corrective and preventive actions. If you... Possess a Bachelor’s degree in Logistics, Supply Chain, Engineering, or a related field, with minimum to no experience, or hold a minimum of four years of experience in manufacturing, logistics, or supply chain environments, with a HS diploma. Demonstrate a strong commitment to safety by proactively identifying and addressing unsafe conditions and adhering to established procedures. Exhibit the ability to work effectively in a fast-paced environment with limited supervision. Hold experience with SAP systems, with additional proficiency is preferred. Business Development Manager -  Atlantic Canada New Brunswick, NewFoundland, Nova Scotia, and Prince Edward Island ** Field Role/ Atlantic Canada / Company Car provided We are looking for a Business Development Manager to join our Refinish team in Atlantic Canada. Come create chemistry with us! BASF Automotive Refinish Coatings Solutions offers high-quality refinish paint brands and paint-related products to body shops from around the world. Our solutions are approved by the world’s leading automotive manufacturers for the repair of their vehicles. We provide collision centers with a vast array of solutions that help drive their business performance and efficiency. At the heart of our business are our people and our trainers who take care of our customers’ needs, including car painting skills, advanced body shop management solutions, and sophisticated management solutions. As a Business Development Manager based in Atlantic Canada with the ability to travel 50% of the time, you will work with automotive collision repair shops and distribution partners to grow our market share. As a Business Development Manager you create chemistry by... Executing the business strategy and driving sales growth in the local markets through identifying customers and market needs within your assigned market responsibility. Increasing sales growth by developing market potential through forecasting, lead generation, qualification, closing sales and recommending new products and services (70% of the role will be focused on adding new business). Managing existing relationships and developing new ones. Establishing trust and value in our ever-changing industry. Retaining business and driving sales growth. If you... Have a Bachelor’s Degree (preferred).  HS Diploma is acceptable with relevant work experience. Are team-focused and experienced in field sales, territory management, customer relations Have experience in industrial sales (beneficial), the collision industry (a plus), strong interpersonal skills, robust relationship-building and value-based selling skills Are a solid professional who will value our current customer base while also being aggressive to win new BASF business Create your own chemistry with you@BASF Adding value to our customers begins with adding value to you. you@BASF is the suite of benefits, perks, programs and unique opportunities we offer to support you – the whole you – in all stages of your life and career. With you@BASF, you create your own chemistry. The total rewards that you receive as a BASF employee go way beyond a paycheck. From competitive health and insurance plans to robust retirement benefits that include company-matching contributions, to making sure you never stop learning, we believe investing in you is investing in our success. Working for a large, global organization, you’ll have a chance to grow professionally and personally, expand your network and build a rewarding and dynamic career.  We also provide engaging learning and development opportunities to help you make the most of your talents and your role. About us As the world’s leading chemical company, we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! We constantly strive to become an even better workplace. As a result of our commitment to our employees, BASF has earned various awards, including being recognized by Mediacorp in 2025 as one of Canada's Top Employers for eleventh consecutive year, one of the Greater Toronto Area's Top Employers for the twelfth year in a row, and twice being named one of Canada's Top Employers for Young People and Best Diversity Employers. We provide a fulfilling work environment with a strong emphasis on the physical and psychological safety of our employees and the communities we operate in. We are always working to form the best team with an emphasis on diversity, equity, and inclusion and through lifelong learning and development. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy Statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request personal or financial information of any kind via either text message or direct message on any social media platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never offer a candidate a position and request a cheque for equipment or any other type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact myhr@basf.com to report fraud. Pay Transparency BASF is committed to pay transparency practices. The competitive Pay Range for this role is $83,000 - $90,000. Actual pay will be determined based on education, certifications, experience, and other job-related factors permitted by law. Inclusion and Equal Opportunity Employment BASF is dedicated to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace where our employees can be their authentic selves. We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, place of origin, ethnic origin, colour, religion/creed, sex/pregnancy, marital status, family status, disability status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by law. We seek to make our recruitment process accessible for applicants with disabilities. If you require accommodations or support throughout the recruitment process, please contact ta-canada@basf.com with the subject line “Recruitment Accommodation Request”. Customer Site Service Rep I Customer Site Service Representative I Location: Newaygo, MI Come create chemistry with us! BASF’s Coatings division is a global expert in the development, production and marketing of innovative and sustainable automotive OEM and refinish coatings, decorative paints as well as applied surface treatments for metal, plastic and glass substrates in a wide range of industries. This portfolio is supplemented by "Beyond Paint Solutions", which enables new applications with innovative surfaces. We are looking for a professional like you to join our team! As a Customer Site Service Representative, you will support the daily operation of BASF products in Customer Automotive Assembly Plants by providing on-site technical and application knowledge. The role responds to customer requirements, enforces product quality/performance specification, and supports new product/application development. You will provide on-site expertise of product performance including color management, defect identification, and corrective action if necessary. S/he will develop and support customer relationships. This role supports the efforts of a BASF sales/technical team to assure value to the customer for product technical support, problem investigation, analysis and resolution. The position assures product performance meets or exceeds customer expectations and supports business objectives to be our customer’s supplier of choice. The incumbent will demonstrate technical and professional expertise to enhance BASF’s reputation with the customer. S/he will integrate within the business and technical community to bring additional value to ECO coatings customers. The role controls key performance indicators within product service environments to drive problem resolution and product improvement initiatives critical to customer performance expectations. As a Customer Site Service Rep I, you create chemistry by... Supporting the daily operation of BASF products in Customer Automotive Assembly Plants. Providing on-site technical and application expertise of product performance including color management, defect identification, and corrective action if necessary. Assuring product performance meets or exceeds customer expectations and supports business objectives to be our customer’s supplier of choice. Responding to customer requirements, enforcing product quality/performance specification, and supporting new product/application development. Supports the efforts of a BASF sales/technical team to assure value to the customer for product technical support, problem investigation, analysis, and resolution. Managing existing relationships and developing new ones. Acting as a capable leader and mentor to coach and develop team members as well as providing ready support for other facilities and customer locations. Developing monthly scorecards and identifying opportunities for continuous improvement for presentation to management. Being a subject matter expert in at least one technology of the coating process i.e.. Ecoat, prime, basecoat or clearcoat. Maintaining the database for smart chart and color care, inventory, and forecasting. Available 24/7 as you will be the sole representative for this account If you have... High school diploma required, advanced degree preferred. 2+ years of automotive coatings or equivalent industry expertise; ability to utilize your advanced knowledge of the painting process and paint applications. Ability to develop and grow customer relationships, focusing on building a positive rapport with customers. Utilizing your inventory control/ERP knowledge you will collect and analyze data and apply results to make material and/or process recommendations. Your strong attention to detail will allow you to continuously inspect and evaluate topcoat defects. Your excellent interpersonal skills will help you with interactions with the team and management at our customer facility. Your strong leadership ability will allow you to run a shift on your own with minimal supervision. Prior experience in a manufacturing environment will help you complete instrument updates and calibrations as well as ancillary paint processes such as spot repair and workability trial processes. description:
