Automation Engineer Greenville, OH At BASF, we create chemistry through the power of connected minds. By balancing economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility, we are building a more sustainable future through chemistry. As the world’s leading chemical company, we help our customers in nearly every industry meet the current and future needs of society through science and innovation. We provide a challenging and rewarding work environment with a strong emphasis on process safety, as well as the safety of our employees and the communities we operate in and are always working to form the best team—especially from within, through an emphasis on lifelong learning and development. And we are constantly striving to become an even better place to work. BASF has been recognized by Forbes Magazine as one of America’s Best Employers. Come join us on our journey to create solutions for a sustainable future! Automation Engineer– Greenville, OH Where the Chemistry Happens We are seeking a professional like you to perform daily responsibilities that include control system programming, including base logic, graphics, and batch programming.  You will lead life-cycle strategy/planning efforts for site control systems and training efforts for site automation.  You will also work closely with Operations to execute programming modifications to de-bottleneck equipment and improve plant performance and reliability and ensure that all shifts are able to execute the operating plan daily, as intended. Additionally, you will lead annual functional certification of site automated interlock checkout, support project engineers during the commissioning phase of capital projects involving automation and utilize your technical background and troubleshooting techniques to improve processes and product quality. Lastly, you will provide support for the Data Historians, ensure plant changes or modifications are captured and documented as part of the PSM "Management of Change", and provide technical support for risk assessments on Management of Changes to ensure changes do not jeopardize the safety of plant. Safety Instrumented System (SIS) Engineer certification or familiarization with safety instrumented systems is highly preferred. You Create Chemistry By... Maintaining the health of DCS system hardware and software. Providing technical support and troubleshooting expertise to the daily Operational Team for plant problems that arise. Making yourself available to be on-call and respond to any after-hour event impacting production or EHS. Providing technical and leadership support in root cause failure analysis for production, EHS, and quality incidents. Identifying Lean opportunities and support implementation. Partnering with the Production Supervisor to maintain safe operation and reliability of the plant. Demonstrating your strong understanding of Process Safety Concepts and their applications on a daily basis.  Additionally, you will comply with OSHA and plant specific safety procedures. Executing small- to medium-sized capital projects related to control systems (DCS, PLC, SPLC) will ensure the project and team meet milestones, strategically mitigate risks, solve problems, and increase value. Guiding third-party process control engineers engaged in project support and maintenance support activities. If you have... Bachelor’s degree in chemical engineering or automation engineering. Advanced degrees will be considered as relevant experience. 7+ years of experience in delivering and maintaining process automation and control systems Experience in plant operations in a fast-paced PSM chemical manufacturing environment. Knowledge of chemical unit operations and equipment, as well as basic instrumentation, controls, and automation. Experience with ABB 800xa (800M controller preferred) and at least one SIS platform (e.g., Triconex, HIMA) Have the ability to quickly learn and analyze complex chemical processes, identify opportunities for improvement, effectively organize and collaborate with cross-functional teams, and present a compelling business case for change to operational managers. Your strong interpersonal skills and excellent written and verbal communication skills will be essential as you partner with the Production Supervisor to maintain safe operation and reliability of the plant. Research Technician I Wyandotte, MI We are looking for a Research Technician I to join our Performance Materials team in Wyandotte. Come create chemistry with us! The Performance Materials division is at the forefront of the much-needed sustainability transformation in plastics. Our experts co-create products with customers to bring innovations to major industry sectors such as transportation, consumer goods, industrial applications, and construction.These solutions contribute to a sustainable future by pushing the boundaries in thermal resistance, robustness and lightweight applications. You will be a member of the PM Research and Development Group supporting R&D and various polyurethane development groups.  You will work with other team members to ensure all projects move forward according to the scheduled activities. You will be responsible for blending various chemical into formulation for machine and outside customer trials . "This position is part of a job family with multiple levels and will be filled at a level that is commensurate with the candidate's qualifications and experience. " During your day shift as a Research Technician I, you will Conduct research, testing, and data collection related to polyurethane processing and product development. Troubleshoot and resolve mechanical issues related to polyurethane processing equipment, ensuring optimal performance. Collaborate with engineers and scientists to develop and improve polyurethane formulations, processes, and technologies. Maintain, operate, and repair laboratory equipment, including mixers, pumps, and mix heads used in polyurethane processing. Provide technical support to the development and production teams to enhance process efficiency and product quality. Document experimental procedures, results, and observations, ensuring accurate records are kept. Perform routine maintenance on lab equipment and assist with equipment upgrades and modifications as needed. Ensure compliance with safety standards and regulatory requirements in all laboratory and processing activities. If you have... Minimum a High School Diploma or equivalent. 2 - 5 years of hands-on experience in polyurethane processing and production. 2 - 5 years of experience in troubleshooting and maintaining polyurethane processing equipment or other mechanical experience. Understanding of chemical processing techniques, including mixing, molding, and curing. The ability to operate mobile equipment, hydraulic presses, and lift a minimum of 50lbs. Familiarity and experience with specific laboratory equipment such as Karl Fischer this would be a plus. 2- 5 years of experience with low pressure, high pressure and continuous polyurethane processing equipment (preferred). Process Solutions Specialist, Passenger Cars Whitehouse, OH We are looking for a Process Solutions Specialist to join our Automotive Refinish team in Whitehouse, OH . Come create chemistry with us! BASF Automotive Refinish Coatings Solutions (ECR) offers high-quality refinish paint brands and paint-related products to body shops from around the world. Our solutions are approved by the world’s leading automotive manufacturers for the repair of their vehicles. We provide collision centers with a vast array of solutions that help drive their business performance and efficiency.  At the heart of our business are our people and our trainers who take care of our customers’ needs including car painting skills, advanced body shop management solutions, and sophisticated management solutions. As a member of the ECR Process Applications team, you will be responsible for application evaluations of global and local products and systems with high focus on efficiencies and customer solutions with globally harmonized concepts. You will drive the implementation and development of competencies and skillsets for BASF and external personnel with technologies, products and process knowledge including training programs and methods according to strategic business objectives. The Process Solution Specialist role is critical in executing BASF’s strategy to create new products and process solutions as a competitive advantage and will require an innovative thinker who can drive new digital solutions that elevate our products and processes and add value for our customers. To succeed in this role, you must possess strong technical skills in the automotive refinish industry and general knowledge of business operations in the collision repair industry. Travel will vary from 10-20%. As a Process Solutions Specialist, Passenger Cars, you create chemistry by... Conducting application evaluations of global & local products and systems (including competitor benchmark products, following global process application methods and measures to achieve product development technical profiles) , determining industry best process efficiencies, supporting tenders &/or approvals for OEM, MSO and other target groups for business development and investigating complaints of Own Manufactured Product (OMP) and other BASF products and systems Coordinating criteria, scope, method and procedures for field trials of global & local product development projects to validate market acceptance prior to launch, following global methods and reporting templates. Capturing all application evaluation and field trial related data, summarizing outcomes and findings in global reporting templates and databases, and timely reporting of all activities. Supporting marketing with technical and process content for creation and modification of BASF, OEM, MSO branded customer training content. Implementing skills development, practical training and education seminars, to develop sustainable local expertise with Train-the-Trainer concepts by coaching internal and external technical team & trainers, and field technicians on products, process, application and repair methods. Marketing launch support by implementing product, process and benefits know-how and evaluation methods and measures to undertake competitor benchmarks for local sustainable expertise following Train-the-Trainer concepts. Setting market field support agendas with strong market up focus and global strategic requirements. Conducting field support activities, creating clear actions aligned with market and sustainable local expertise requirements. Summarizing outcomes and findings in global reporting templates, timely reporting of all market field support activities. If you... NA Product Manager for Oxo-C4 Alcohols Houston, TX (Hybrid) We are looking for a NA Product Manager for Oxo-C4 Alcohols to join our Petrochemicals team in Houston, TX. Where the chemistry happens… BASF’s Petrochemical division is the starting point of BASF's petrochemical value creation worldwide, also known as “the heart of the Verbund,” representing the world's largest chemical markets. Together, let us keep the heart beating one creative solution at a time! We are seeking a professional like you for our NA Product Manager Oxo-C4 Alcohols in the Petrochemicals business unit.  In this role, you will be responsible for pricing and margin management, development and maintenance of financial forecasts, demand forecasts, operational planning, supply planning, inventory targets, cost reduction initiatives and business process improvement. As the key central point of contact for daily operations of the business and with a focus on the short to mid-term outlook, you will work effectively in close collaboration with our Manufacturing, Sales, Supply Chain, Marketing, Controlling, and Technical Service teams, as well as Customer Care & Purchasing, to drive activities and initiatives to meet business targets and objectives. Come create chemistry with us! As a NA Product Manager for Oxo-C4 Alcohols, you create chemistry by... Managing pricing for the product portfolio focusing on margin management to meet and/or exceed business profitability targets as outlined in the annual budget. Ensuring optimized use of our asset base, coproducers and tollers, while balancing working capital and an internal/external customer service/focus. Implementing pricing initiatives based on market dynamics (raw materials, supply/demand balances, competitive situations, etc.) in conjunction with sales and marketing teams. Analyzing the Oxo Alcohols Monthly P&L and Cost Centers, including development of commentary for communication of results, and implementation of resulting actions to meet business objectives. Maintaining and developing relationships with Co-Producers and manage commercial activities including tolling, swaps, exchanges, sales and purchase activities. Identifying and capitalizing on opportunities with your BASF business counterparts in other regions where we have import / export relationships. Commiting to our continuous improvement culture by completing training and implementing continuous improvement initiatives for the business. Supporting the Americas i.e. North and South America Oxo Alcohols Marketing and Sales Team for development of and implementation of milestones and deliverables within the Oxo strategy, including portfolio evaluations, market intelligence and tactical and longer-term projects. If you... Have a bachelor’s degree in chemistry, business or related degree or equivalent. Possess at least 3 years of experience in product management or related field to improve profitability of your portfolio related to Manufacturing environments. Have a master’s in Business Administration (MBA) (preferred). Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future. In addition to competitive compensation and benefits, BASF provides you with access to a wide range of elements to help you be your best. It’s what we call you@BASF . We are committed to providing benefits, programs, and opportunities that support our employees’ overall well-being, personal growth, and a safe, collaborative, and inclusive work environment. Just some of the many benefits we offer include: Flexible work arrangements whenever possible Highly competitive retirement savings plan with company match and investment options Well-being programs that include comprehensive mental health support for you and your household family members Family forming benefits (fertility, adoption and surrogacy reimbursement, maternity/parental leave, and more) Back-up child and elder care with discount programs for families of all ages and stages Mentoring and career development opportunities that allow you to share, learn, and thrive Matching gifts program that allows you to deepen the impact of your contributions to qualified charities. Employee crisis support for when the unexpected happens Access to our BASF wine cellar, employee discounts, and much more! About us As one of the largest chemical companies in North America we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! At BASF we empower our employees with the tools, guidance and opportunities they need to advance and succeed in work and life. Giving you the support you need to be your best and fulfill your personal ambitions is what helps us create chemistry. After all, our success is linked to yours. Whatever path you envision, BASF is a great place to build a rewarding, successful career. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. Construction Manager Staff Geismar, LA - Houston, TX - Freeport, TX (Hybrid) This role requires the availability to travel 75% of the time. We are looking for a Construction Manager Staff to join our Global Engineering team in Geismar, LA, Houston, TX or Freeport, TX Come create chemistry with us! At BASF, we create chemistry through the power of connected minds. As the world’s leading chemical company, we help our customers in nearly every industry meet the current and future needs of society through science and innovation. The Global Engineering team leverages our technical know-how and expertise to deliver solutions that enable safe, sustainable, reliable, and cost-competitive production. As a Construction Manager Staff, your primary goal is to provide technical support for construction, commissioning, and start-up activities. You'll manage construction contractors, focusing on safety, quality, schedule, and cost, while addressing technical issues that arise during execution. Your role requires strong knowledge of construction management practices, technical standards, and the economic factors related to large capital projects. As a Construction Manager Staff, you create chemistry by... Overseeing the daily construction execution, commissioning, and start-up support for Large Capital Projects, along with a multidisciplinary understanding of construction for installing engineered process equipment in the Chemical and Petrochemical industry. Managing construction productivity and scheduling performance while investigating the causes of any unsatisfactory results. This includes understanding pre-constructability aspects and developing plans for construction logistics, quality management, sequencing, and site considerations. Promoting a strong safety culture by implementing the Construction EHS Plan for projects and proactively addressing potential disputes to prevent construction claims. Assisting the Project Team in reviewing and authorizing field changes to mitigate additional costs. Collaborating with internal and external stakeholders to ensure that scope, budget, and schedule are effectively planned and managed throughout the construction execution process. Understands and utilizes digital tools to create efficiencies in construction. If you... Hold a bachelor's degree in an Engineering field or Construction Management. Have 10+ years of construction experience in large petrochemical or oil and gas processing facilities. Possess a results-oriented mindset while fostering strong working relationships and collaborating effectively across all levels of the organization. Have the capacity to think strategically to minimize risks, address challenges, and maximize value. Demonstrates strong skills in leadership, safety standards, contractor management, cost control, schedule development and management, as well as construction practices. Are available for extended travel as needed to support capital projects and construction efforts. Flexible Raw Material Technical Service Representative Wyandotte, MI (On-site) Annual bonus We are looking for a Flexible Raw Material Technical Service Representative to join our Monomers team in Wyandotte, MI. Come create chemistry with us! BASF Monomers is a leading supplier of isocyanates, inorganics, polyamides, and ammonia. Our products are used in many downstream applications including the production of foams, adhesives, textiles, automobiles, food production, paper, packaging, wire, cable, clothing, and so much more. Monomers go into the production of countless household and industrial goods and make a positive impact on people's lives every day. As an essential business in BASF’s overall Verbund (interconnected value chain) system, Team Monomers is committed to help our customers reach their sustainability targets by expanding our portfolio of lower CO2 footprint products and circular solutions. As a Flexible Raw Material Technical Service Representative, you will be responsible for flexible PU development, product launches, and technical support for existing and new BASF customers. You will use your demonstrated technical and troubleshooting skills to facilitate technical support, product innovation, while working individually and in a team setting in a safe and timely manner. As a Flexible Raw Material Technical Service Representative, you create chemistry by… Supporting technically flexible polyurethane products (isocyanates and polyols) for third party raw materials market in USA, Canada, Mexico, and Central America/Caribbean. Developing, managing, prioritizing, and driving to completion automotive/molded and slab foam technical development projects. Acting as the BASF face to the PU industry – with strong customer facing interactions that include development of training webinars, industry presentations, and engagement with industry associations (American Chemistry Council – CPI, Polyurethane Foam Association, Foam Expo, etc.) Supporting the technical side of sustainability efforts for polyurethane flexible foam applications developing expertise in BASF routes for sustainable PU (BMB, reduced PCF, recycled materials, bio-based options). Working closely with new business development, you will identify new ideas for innovation (related to product, application, digitalization or sustainability), define and start projects, assemble and manage internal and external resources, drive initiatives to full completion and ensure execution in compliance with project timelines, as well as safety and regulatory requirements. Being responsible for the technical launches of new products and technologies, with a focus on aligning value propositions with market needs and optimizing value creation for BASF through innovation. If you… Hold a bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Chemistry or other applied sciences. Have relevant experience in technical, customer-facing, business development or other similar roles, typically gained over 8+ years. Polyurethane or urethane expertise will be a plus and considered in evaluating overall qualifications. Possess strong laboratory skills. Shipper Attapulgus, GA (Onsite) We are looking for a Shipper to join our Performance Chemicals team in Attapulgus, GA. Come create chemistry with us! As an innovative partner, BASF’s Performance Chemicals division offers chemicals for various customer industries, such as plastics, automotive, refining, lubricants, oilfield and mining. Our highly qualified and experienced team with outstanding market knowledge as well as our innovation platform and application know-how ensure our technological competence to provide excellent solutions to our customers. As a Shipper, you will support the safe and efficient movement, handling, packaging, storage, and shipment of raw materials and finished products. In this role, you will operate logistics and material handling equipment, support additive production and shipping activities, manage inventory and warehouse processes, and ensure compliance with operational, quality, environmental, health, and safety requirements. During your 12 hour shift as a Shipper, you will Perform shared logistics duties including participating in housekeeping audits, following Blending Coordinator instructions and notes, completing safety shift inspection checklists, reporting repair needs, working with other Logistics Operators and Team Leaders to prioritize workload, and maintaining a clean and safe work environment. Understand, monitor, and operate within process safety operational guidelines, complete required process safety training, report process safety incidents, access SDS forms and EHS procedures, and follow written and verbal instructions while working independently to task completion. Direct truck drivers from staging areas and truck stops using telephones, CB radios, and plant radios, including properly spotting trucks according to procedures. Safely operate, maintain, perform minor repairs, and make proper adjustments to associated equipment, while properly labeling, storing, and handling materials used in logistics and production operations. Support export shipping activities by operating forklifts, pre-loader vehicles, blender loading systems, tote bin loaders, scales, chutes, and conveyors, as well as filling, labeling, staging, storing, and loading super sacks, tote bins, sea bulk containers, and domestic FCC shipments. Manufacture and package additive products by following process orders and procedures, weighing and blending components, selecting appropriate raw material lots, storing materials by lot and grade, sampling and labeling products, generating SDS shipping labels, and packaging additive production campaigns for transfer. Manage inventory and warehouse activities including locating and inventorying additives and raw materials, controlling inventory through computer and scanner systems, receiving packaging supplies and materials, verifying quantities against receiving documentation, and receiving, inspecting, repairing, and storing returned tote bins. Participate as a member of the Attapulgus Site Confined Space Rescue Team, utilize respiratory protection as required, maintain annual fit-testing certification, and perform additional duties assigned by supervision. If you... Hold a valid driver's license. Have the ability to safely operate, maintain, perform minor repairs, and make proper adjustments on associated equipment in assigned work areas. Are able to follow written and verbal instructions and work independently to task completion. Can read, write, count, and perform accurate arithmetic calculations using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division. Have the ability to use computers to access procedures, SDS information, inventory systems, shipping documentation, email, and other operational resources. Are able to properly label, store, receive, inspect, and manage materials in accordance with site procedures. Can safely operate a forklift and successfully complete required forklift training. Are able to use normally available resources such as Process Orders, Procedures, and notes to perform required job duties and complete qualification sign-offs. description:

Global Marketing Manager - Beauty Care Solutions Remote - US We are looking for a Marketing Manager to join our Beauty Care Solutions team in Care Chemicals. Come create chemistry with us! BASF’s Care Chemicals division is a globally leading supplier to the cosmetics, detergents and cleaning industries. We also offer solutions for technical applications and crop protection. Together with our customers, we create innovative solutions to meet the current and future needs of society more sustainably. We contribute to the long-term success of our customers’ brands with a broad range of products and concepts via our global network of production and development site. As a Global Marketing Manager – Actives, Beauty Care Solutions (BCS), you will be instrumental in managing innovation projects, launching new products, developing global communication strategy and driving BCS visibility. Additionally, you will support local Americas organization in developing and executing go‑to‑market strategies for innovative cosmetic ingredients to drive customer engagement and revenue growth. Your work will directly translate BASF’s scientific expertise into differentiated value propositions that strengthen our position in the beauty market. As a Global Marketing Manager - Beauty Care Solutions, you create chemistry by… Using global and regional knowledge to leverage portfolio and innovation​ Driving Beauty Care Solutions' communication activities both globally and locally Supporting the strategy of Beauty Care Solution globally, with specific insights on Americas region, including market and competition analysis, potential partnerships and go-to-market approach. Managing the innovation- identifying market needs and managing ideation campaigns with the aim of creating new project briefs and feeding the innovation pipeline. Work with the project manager on new projects (market analysis, business case and go to market approach) Launching new products - prepare the launch package together with project manager, define pricing strategy, value proposition, competitive advantage, product positioning, and communication plan. Follow up on newly launched products and adjust accordingly. Developing and supporting business opportunities, as well as market presence in Americas If you… Have a bachelor’s degree in biology, biochemistry, chemistry, chemical engineering, marketing, or a related field. Possess 3+ years experience in personal care, actives, or specialty ingredients marketing, product management, or technical sales, with demonstrated exposure to cosmetic or formulation-driven markets. Have a strong ability to translate scientific or technical data into compelling customer value propositions and marketing narratives. Demonstrate strong cross-functional collaboration skills, with experience working effectively with R&D, Sales, and global teams. Show a commercial and execution-focused mindset, with the ability to manage multiple projects, meet deadlines, and contribute to business growth. Exhibit strong communication and analytical skills, with the ability to synthesize market insights and clearly communicate to both technical and commercial audiences. Create your own chemistry with you@BASF At BASF, you will have the chance to do meaningful work towards building a more sustainable future.
